```text
1	import { neo4jDriver, neo4jDatabase } from "../utils/neo4j";
2	
3	async function runQuery(
4	  cypher: string,
5	  params: Record<string, unknown> = {},
6	) {
7	  const session = neo4jDriver.session({
8	    database: neo4jDatabase ?? undefined,
9	  });
10	  try {
11	    const result = await session.run(cypher, params);
12	    return result.records;
13	  } finally {
14	    await session.close();
15	  }
16	}
17	
18	export async function ensureIndices(): Promise<void> {
19	  const statements = [
20	    `CREATE CONSTRAINT entity_id_unique IF NOT EXISTS
21	       FOR (e:Entity) REQUIRE e.entityId IS UNIQUE`,
22	    `CREATE INDEX entity_user_normname_idx IF NOT EXISTS
23	       FOR (e:Entity) ON (e.userId, e.normalizedName)`,
24	    `CREATE CONSTRAINT episode_id_unique IF NOT EXISTS
25	       FOR (e:Episode) REQUIRE e.episodeId IS UNIQUE`,
26	    `CREATE INDEX episode_user_idx IF NOT EXISTS
27	       FOR (e:Episode) ON (e.userId)`,
28	    `CREATE CONSTRAINT fact_id_unique IF NOT EXISTS
29	       FOR ()-[f:FACT]-() REQUIRE f.factId IS UNIQUE`,
30	  ];
31	  for (const cypher of statements) {
32	    await runQuery(cypher);
33	  }
34	}
35	
36	export type GraphEntity = {
37	  entityId: string;
38	  userId: string;
39	  name: string;
40	  normalizedName: string;
41	  type: string;
42	  summary: string;
43	};
44	
45	export function normalizeEntityName(name: string): string {
46	  return name.trim().toLowerCase().replace(/\s+/g, " ");
47	}
48	
49	export async function findEntitiesByNormalizedNames(
50	  userId: string,
51	  normalizedNames: string[],
52	): Promise<Map<string, GraphEntity>> {
53	  if (normalizedNames.length === 0) return new Map();
54	
55	  const records = await runQuery(
56	    `
57	    MATCH (e:Entity { userId: $userId })
58	    WHERE e.normalizedName IN $names
59	    RETURN e
60	    `,
61	    { userId, names: normalizedNames },
62	  );
63	
64	  const map = new Map<string, GraphEntity>();
65	  for (const record of records) {
66	    const p = record.get("e").properties;
67	    map.set(p.normalizedName, {
68	      entityId: p.entityId,
69	      userId: p.userId,
70	      name: p.name,
71	      normalizedName: p.normalizedName,
72	      type: p.type,
73	      summary: p.summary,
74	    });
75	  }
76	  return map;
77	}
78	
79	export async function upsertEntity(entity: GraphEntity): Promise<void> {
80	  await runQuery(
81	    `
82	    MERGE (e:Entity { entityId: $entityId })
83	    ON CREATE SET
84	      e.userId = $userId,
85	      e.name = $name,
86	      e.normalizedName = $normalizedName,
87	      e.type = $type,
88	      e.summary = $summary,
89	      e.createdAt = datetime(),
90	      e.updatedAt = datetime()
91	    ON MATCH SET
92	      e.name = $name,
93	      e.summary = $summary,
94	      e.type = $type,
95	      e.updatedAt = datetime()
96	    `,
97	    entity,
98	  );
99	}
100	
101	export async function upsertEntities(entities: GraphEntity[]): Promise<void> {
102	  if (entities.length === 0) return;
103	  await runQuery(
104	    `
105	    UNWIND $entities AS ent
106	    MERGE (e:Entity { entityId: ent.entityId })
107	    ON CREATE SET
108	      e.userId = ent.userId,
109	      e.name = ent.name,
110	      e.normalizedName = ent.normalizedName,
111	      e.type = ent.type,
112	      e.summary = ent.summary,
113	      e.createdAt = datetime(),
114	      e.updatedAt = datetime()
115	    ON MATCH SET
116	      e.name = ent.name,
117	      e.summary = ent.summary,
118	      e.type = ent.type,
119	      e.updatedAt = datetime()
120	    `,
121	    { entities },
122	  );
123	}
124	
125	export type GraphEpisode = {
126	  episodeId: string;
127	  userId: string;
128	  sessionId: string;
129	  actor: string;
130	  content: string;
131	  occurredAt: string;
132	};
133	
134	export async function createEpisodeNode(
135	  episode: GraphEpisode,
136	): Promise<void> {
137	  await runQuery(
138	    `
139	    MERGE (e:Episode { episodeId: $episodeId })
140	    ON CREATE SET
141	      e.userId = $userId,
142	      e.sessionId = $sessionId,
143	      e.actor = $actor,
144	      e.content = $content,
145	      e.occurredAt = datetime($occurredAt),
146	      e.createdAt = datetime()
147	    `,
148	    episode,
149	  );
150	}
151	
152	export async function linkEpisodeToEntities(
153	  episodeId: string,
154	  entityIds: string[],
155	  occurredAt: string,
156	): Promise<void> {
157	  if (entityIds.length === 0) return;
158	  await runQuery(
159	    `
160	    MATCH (ep:Episode { episodeId: $episodeId })
161	    UNWIND $entityIds AS eid
162	    MATCH (ent:Entity { entityId: eid })
163	    MERGE (ep)-[m:MENTIONS]->(ent)
164	    ON CREATE SET m.timestamp = datetime($occurredAt)
165	    `,
166	    { episodeId, entityIds, occurredAt },
167	  );
168	}
169	
170	export type GraphFact = {
171	  factId: string;
172	  sourceEntityId: string;
173	  targetEntityId: string;
174	  relationType: string;
175	  factText: string;
176	  validFrom: string;
177	  validUntil: string | null;
178	  confidence: number;
179	  episodeId: string;
180	};
181	
182	export async function findOpenFactsForSourceAndRelation(
183	  sourceEntityId: string,
184	  relationType: string,
185	): Promise<Array<{ factId: string; targetEntityId: string }>> {
186	  const records = await runQuery(
187	    `
188	    MATCH (a:Entity { entityId: $sourceEntityId })
189	          -[f:FACT { relationType: $relationType }]->
190	          (b:Entity)
191	    WHERE f.validUntil IS NULL
192	    RETURN f.factId AS factId, b.entityId AS targetEntityId
193	    `,
194	    { sourceEntityId, relationType },
195	  );
196	  return records.map((r) => ({
197	    factId: r.get("factId") as string,
198	    targetEntityId: r.get("targetEntityId") as string,
199	  }));
200	}
201	
202	export async function closeFact(
203	  factId: string,
204	  validUntil: string,
205	): Promise<void> {
206	  await runQuery(
207	    `
208	    MATCH ()-[f:FACT { factId: $factId }]->()
209	    SET f.validUntil = datetime($validUntil)
210	    `,
211	    { factId, validUntil },
212	  );
213	}
214	
215	export type ExpandedFact = {
216	  factId: string;
217	  sourceId: string;
218	  sourceName: string;
219	  sourceType: string;
220	  relationType: string;
221	  targetId: string;
222	  targetName: string;
223	  targetType: string;
224	  factText: string;
225	  validFrom: string;
226	  validUntil: string | null;
227	  confidence: number;
228	  hop: number;
229	};
230	
231	export async function expandFromIds(
232	  userId: string,
233	  input: { entityIds: string[]; factIds: string[]; asOf: string },
234	): Promise<ExpandedFact[]> {
235	  const { entityIds, factIds, asOf } = input;
236	  if (entityIds.length === 0 && factIds.length === 0) return [];
237	
238	  const records = await runQuery(
239	    `
240	    MATCH (a:Entity { userId: $userId })-[f:FACT]->(b:Entity)
241	    WHERE (
242	      f.factId IN $factIds
243	      OR a.entityId IN $entityIds
244	      OR b.entityId IN $entityIds
245	    )
246	    AND f.validFrom <= datetime($asOf)
247	    AND coalesce(f.validUntil, datetime('9999-01-01')) >= datetime($asOf)
248	    RETURN
249	      f.factId AS factId,
250	      a.entityId AS sourceId, a.name AS sourceName, a.type AS sourceType,
251	      f.relationType AS relationType,
252	      b.entityId AS targetId, b.name AS targetName, b.type AS targetType,
253	      f.factText AS factText,
254	      toString(f.validFrom) AS validFrom,
255	      toString(f.validUntil) AS validUntil,
256	      coalesce(f.confidence, 1.0) AS confidence,
257	      CASE WHEN f.factId IN $factIds THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS hop
258	    `,
259	    { userId, factIds, entityIds, asOf },
260	  );
261	
262	  return records.map((r) => ({
263	    factId: r.get("factId") as string,
264	    sourceId: r.get("sourceId") as string,
265	    sourceName: r.get("sourceName") as string,
266	    sourceType: r.get("sourceType") as string,
267	    relationType: r.get("relationType") as string,
268	    targetId: r.get("targetId") as string,
269	    targetName: r.get("targetName") as string,
270	    targetType: r.get("targetType") as string,
271	    factText: r.get("factText") as string,
272	    validFrom: r.get("validFrom") as string,
273	    validUntil: (r.get("validUntil") as string | null) ?? null,
274	    confidence: Number(r.get("confidence")),
275	    hop: Number(r.get("hop")),
276	  }));
277	}
278	
279	export async function upsertFact(fact: GraphFact): Promise<void> {
280	  await runQuery(
281	    `
282	    MATCH (a:Entity { entityId: $sourceEntityId })
283	    MATCH (b:Entity { entityId: $targetEntityId })
284	    MERGE (a)-[f:FACT { factId: $factId }]->(b)
285	    ON CREATE SET
286	      f.relationType = $relationType,
287	      f.factText = $factText,
288	      f.validFrom = datetime($validFrom),
289	      f.validUntil = CASE WHEN $validUntil IS NULL THEN NULL ELSE datetime($validUntil) END,
290	      f.confidence = $confidence,
291	      f.episodeId = $episodeId,
292	      f.createdAt = datetime()
293	    `,
294	    fact,
295	  );
296	}
297
```

```text
1	import { openai } from "../utils/openai";
2	import { pineconeIndex } from "../utils/pinecone";
3	import type { ResolvedEntity } from "./resolver.service";
4	import type { TemporalFact } from "./temporal.service";
5	
6	const EMBEDDING_MODEL = "text-embedding-3-small";
7	
8	export async function embed(text: string): Promise<number[]> {
9	  const response = await openai.embeddings.create({
10	    model: EMBEDDING_MODEL,
11	    input: text,
12	  });
13	  return response.data[0].embedding;
14	}
15	
16	export async function embedMany(texts: string[]): Promise<number[][]> {
17	  if (texts.length === 0) return [];
18	  const response = await openai.embeddings.create({
19	    model: EMBEDDING_MODEL,
20	    input: texts,
21	  });
22	  return response.data.map((d) => d.embedding);
23	}
24	
25	export async function upsertEntityVectors(
26	  entities: ResolvedEntity[],
27	): Promise<number> {
28	  const newOnes = entities.filter((e) => e.isNew);
29	  if (newOnes.length === 0) return 0;
30	
31	  const texts = newOnes.map((e) => `${e.name}: ${e.summary}`);
32	  const vectors = await embedMany(texts);
33	
34	  const records = newOnes.map((e, i) => ({
35	    id: e.entityId,
36	    values: vectors[i],
37	    metadata: {
38	      type: "entity",
39	      userId: e.userId,
40	      neo4jId: e.entityId,
41	      name: e.name,
42	      entityType: e.type,
43	    },
44	  }));
45	
46	  await pineconeIndex.upsert({ records });
47	  return records.length;
48	}
49	
50	export async function upsertFactVectors(
51	  userId: string,
52	  facts: TemporalFact[],
53	): Promise<number> {
54	  if (facts.length === 0) return 0;
55	
56	  const texts = facts.map((f) => f.factText);
57	  const vectors = await embedMany(texts);
58	
59	  const records = facts.map((f, i) => ({
60	    id: f.factId,
61	    values: vectors[i],
62	    metadata: {
63	      type: "fact",
64	      userId,
65	      neo4jId: f.factId,
66	      sourceId: f.sourceEntityId,
67	      targetId: f.targetEntityId,
68	      relationType: f.relationType,
69	      factText: f.factText,
70	    },
71	  }));
72	
73	  await pineconeIndex.upsert({ records });
74	  return records.length;
75	}
76	
77	export type VectorMatch = {
78	  id: string;
79	  score: number | undefined;
80	  metadata: Record<string, unknown> | undefined;
81	};
82	
83	export async function queryVectors(
84	  text: string,
85	  userId: string,
86	  topK: number = 10,
87	  extraFilter?: Record<string, unknown>,
88	): Promise<VectorMatch[]> {
89	  const vector = await embed(text);
90	  const filter: Record<string, unknown> = { userId };
91	  if (extraFilter) Object.assign(filter, extraFilter);
92	
93	  const result = await pineconeIndex.query({
94	    vector,
95	    topK,
96	    filter,
97	    includeMetadata: true,
98	  });
99	
100	  return (result.matches ?? []).map((m) => ({
101	    id: m.id,
102	    score: m.score,
103	    metadata: m.metadata as Record<string, unknown> | undefined,
104	  }));
105	}
106
```

```text
1	import { and, asc, desc, eq, ne } from "drizzle-orm";
2	import { db } from "../models/db";
3	import { sessions, episodes, processingLogs } from "../models/schema";
4	import { enqueueEpisodeProcessing } from "../queues/ingestion.queue";
5	
6	export class SessionNotFoundError extends Error {
7	  constructor() {
8	    super("SESSION_NOT_FOUND");
9	    this.name = "SessionNotFoundError";
10	  }
11	}
12	
13	export class EpisodeNotFoundError extends Error {
14	  constructor() {
15	    super("EPISODE_NOT_FOUND");
16	    this.name = "EpisodeNotFoundError";
17	  }
18	}
19	
20	export type EpisodeRow = typeof episodes.$inferSelect;
21	export type ProcessingLogRow = typeof processingLogs.$inferSelect;
22	
23	export type CreateEpisodeInput = {
24	  sessionId: string;
25	  actor: "user" | "assistant" | "system";
26	  content: string;
27	  occurredAt?: string;
28	};
29	
30	export async function createEpisode(
31	  userId: string,
32	  input: CreateEpisodeInput,
33	): Promise<EpisodeRow> {
34	  const [session] = await db
35	    .select({ id: sessions.id })
36	    .from(sessions)
37	    .where(and(eq(sessions.id, input.sessionId), eq(sessions.userId, userId)))
38	    .limit(1);
39	
40	  if (!session) {
41	    throw new SessionNotFoundError();
42	  }
43	
44	  const occurredAt = input.occurredAt ? new Date(input.occurredAt) : new Date();
45	
46	  const [row] = await db
47	    .insert(episodes)
48	    .values({
49	      userId,
50	      sessionId: input.sessionId,
51	      actor: input.actor,
52	      content: input.content,
53	      occurredAt,
54	    })
55	    .returning();
56	
57	  await enqueueEpisodeProcessing(row.id);
58	
59	  return row;
60	}
61	
62	export async function getEpisodeWithLogs(
63	  userId: string,
64	  episodeId: string,
65	): Promise<{ episode: EpisodeRow; logs: ProcessingLogRow[] }> {
66	  const [episode] = await db
67	    .select()
68	    .from(episodes)
69	    .where(and(eq(episodes.id, episodeId), eq(episodes.userId, userId)))
70	    .limit(1);
71	
72	  if (!episode) throw new EpisodeNotFoundError();
73	
74	  const logs = await db
75	    .select()
76	    .from(processingLogs)
77	    .where(eq(processingLogs.episodeId, episodeId))
78	    .orderBy(asc(processingLogs.createdAt));
79	
80	  return { episode, logs };
81	}
82	
83	export async function fetchEpisodeById(
84	  episodeId: string,
85	): Promise<EpisodeRow | null> {
86	  const [row] = await db
87	    .select()
88	    .from(episodes)
89	    .where(eq(episodes.id, episodeId))
90	    .limit(1);
91	  return row ?? null;
92	}
93	
94	export type RecentMessage = {
95	  actor: "user" | "assistant" | "system";
96	  content: string;
97	  occurredAt: Date;
98	};
99	
100	export async function getRecentMessages(
101	  sessionId: string,
102	  limit: number,
103	  excludeEpisodeId?: string,
104	): Promise<RecentMessage[]> {
105	  const where = excludeEpisodeId
106	    ? and(eq(episodes.sessionId, sessionId), ne(episodes.id, excludeEpisodeId))
107	    : eq(episodes.sessionId, sessionId);
108	
109	  const rows = await db
110	    .select({
111	      actor: episodes.actor,
112	      content: episodes.content,
113	      occurredAt: episodes.occurredAt,
114	    })
115	    .from(episodes)
116	    .where(where)
117	    .orderBy(desc(episodes.occurredAt))
118	    .limit(limit);
119	
120	  return rows.reverse();
121	}
122
```

```text
1	import type { Context } from "hono";
2	import type { AuthVariables } from "../middleware/auth.middleware";
3	import { ingestSchema } from "../validators/ingest.validator";
4	import {
5	  createEpisode,
6	  SessionNotFoundError,
7	} from "../services/episodes.service";
8	
9	export async function ingest(c: Context<{ Variables: AuthVariables }>) {
10	  const userId = c.get("userId");
11	  const body = await c.req.json().catch(() => null);
12	  const parsed = ingestSchema.safeParse(body);
13	  if (!parsed.success) {
14	    return c.json(
15	      { error: "VALIDATION_ERROR", issues: parsed.error.issues },
16	      400,
17	    );
18	  }
19	
20	  try {
21	    const episode = await createEpisode(userId, parsed.data);
22	    return c.json(
23	      { episodeId: episode.id, status: episode.status },
24	      202,
25	    );
26	  } catch (err) {
27	    if (err instanceof SessionNotFoundError) {
28	      return c.json({ error: "SESSION_NOT_FOUND" }, 404);
29	    }
30	    throw err;
31	  }
32	}
33
```

```text
1	import { Queue } from "bullmq";
2	import { redisConnection } from "./connection";
3	
4	export const INGESTION_QUEUE_NAME = "memory-ingestion";
5	
6	export type IngestionJobData = {
7	  episodeId: string;
8	};
9	
10	export const ingestionQueue = new Queue<IngestionJobData>(
11	  INGESTION_QUEUE_NAME,
12	  {
13	    connection: redisConnection,
14	    defaultJobOptions: {
15	      attempts: 3,
16	      backoff: { type: "exponential", delay: 1000 },
17	      removeOnComplete: { age: 60 * 60 * 24 },
18	      removeOnFail: { age: 60 * 60 * 24 * 7 },
19	    },
20	  },
21	);
22	
23	export async function enqueueEpisodeProcessing(episodeId: string) {
24	  return ingestionQueue.add(
25	    "process-episode",
26	    { episodeId },
27	    { jobId: episodeId },
28	  );
29	}
30
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-11 17:03:04Z)**_

Short answer: **the shape matches the paper, but several specific mechanisms the paper describes are not the same — your pipeline is a simplified rule-based variant of Graphiti, not a faithful port.**

Here's the side-by-side, going step by step through Section 2 of the paper against your worker (`server/src/workers/ingestion.worker.ts`).

## What matches the paper

| Paper concept | Where it lives in your code |
|---|---|
| Three-tier graph: Episode → Entity → Community subgraphs | `neo4j.service.ts` — `:Episode`, `:Entity`, `:Community` labels; `MENTIONS`, `FACT`, `CONTAINS` edges |
| Episodic edges `Ee` connecting episodes to entities | `linkEpisodeToEntities` → `(ep)-[:MENTIONS]->(ent)` (worker step `link_episode_entities`) |
| Raw episodes preserved non-lossy alongside derived semantic data | Postgres `episodes` + Neo4j `:Episode` node (`createEpisodeNode`) |
| Use of last *n* messages for context during entity extraction | `getRecentMessages(sessionId, 6, …)` — paper uses n=4, you use 6 |
| Speaker extracted as an entity participating in facts | `getOrCreateSelfEntity` (you split it out instead of inline, but the effect is the same) |
| Reference timestamp `t_ref` carried through | `episode.occurredAt` → `occurredAtIso` |
| `validFrom` / `validUntil` on edges | `GraphFact.validFrom` / `validUntil` (Cypher property on `:FACT`) |
| Old contradicting edges *closed, not deleted* | `closeFact` sets `validUntil`, never `DELETE` |
| Cypher writes instead of LLM-generated queries (paper §2.2.1) | All writes are pre-defined Cypher |
| Embeddings for entities and facts | `pinecone.service.ts` upserts both |
| `MERGE`-based dedupe via `factId`/`entityId` | Throughout `neo4j.service.ts` |
| Focus on `message` episode type only | `extraction.service.ts` only handles messages — and the paper explicitly scopes its experiments to messages |

## Where the implementation diverges from the paper

**1. Bi-temporal model — only half implemented.** The paper tracks four timestamps: `t_created`, `t_expired` on T′ (transaction time) AND `t_valid`, `t_invalid` on T (event time). You only have `validFrom`/`validUntil` (T). There is no `t'_created` / `t'_expired`. `PLAN.md §12` flags this as a known open decision.

**2. Temporal extraction LLM step is missing.** The paper (§2.2.3 + Appendix 6.1.5) runs an explicit LLM prompt with the fact + `t_ref` to derive `valid_at` / `invalid_at`, handling absolute dates ("June 23, 1912") and relative ones ("two weeks ago"). Your worker step `apply_temporal` just sets `validFrom = episode.occurredAt`. There is no prompt that parses "last summer" or "two weeks ago" out of the fact text. Grep confirms only an entity-extraction prompt and a fact-extraction prompt exist.

**3. Entity resolution is name-normalization, not the paper's hybrid pipeline.** Paper §2.2.1:
- embed entity name into 1024-d vector → cosine search
- *plus* full-text search on name+summary
- *plus* LLM resolution prompt over the candidates

Your `resolveEntities` (`resolver.service.ts:13`) only does `findEntitiesByNormalizedNames` — a lowercase/whitespace match. No embedding lookup, no full-text search, no LLM resolution prompt (even though the paper's Appendix 6.1.2 prompt is exactly that). "Apple" and "Apple Inc." will not be resolved as duplicates; only exact-name re-mentions will.

**4. Edge invalidation is rule-based, not LLM-semantic.** Paper §2.2.3: "The system employs an LLM to compare new edges against *semantically related* existing edges to identify potential contradictions." Your `applyTemporalFacts` (`temporal.service.ts:24`) closes opens via the rule "same `(sourceEntityId, relationType)` and target differs," plus explicit `invalidations` returned by the fact-extraction LLM. There is no semantic comparison against related-but-not-exact-predicate edges. `loves` vs `likes` or `lives_in` vs `resides_in` will not be detected as conflicts — `PLAN.md §12` actually calls this out.

**5. Fact deduplication is missing the paper's hybrid pair-scoped search.** Paper §2.2.2: edge dedup uses "hybrid search for relevant edges constrained to edges existing between the same entity pairs." Your `upsertFact` just `MERGE`s on a fresh `factId` (UUID), so re-mentioning the same triple in a different episode creates a new `:FACT` edge each time. Only the `(source, relation)` open-edge check in `applyTemporalFacts` catches the rare reaffirmation case (and only when the target also matches — `skipped++`). The paper's hyper-edge / pair-scoped semantic dedup is not implemented.

**6. Reflexion step on entity extraction is missing.** Paper §2.2.1: "we employ a reflection technique inspired by reflexion to minimize hallucinations and enhance extraction coverage." Your `extractEntities` is a single one-shot LLM call — no reflection pass.

**7. Communities are not implemented at all.** Paper §2.3 specifies label-propagation community detection, dynamic single-step extension on new node insertion, periodic full refreshes, map-reduce summaries, and community-name embeddings. There's no `community.service.ts`, no clustering job, no `:Community` writes anywhere in the worker, no `CONTAINS` edges created. `PLAN.md` lists communities as step 11 — not built.

**8. Bidirectional index from semantic edges back to episodes is not stored on FACT edges.** Paper §2.1: "Episodes and their derived semantic edges maintain bidirectional indices that track the relationships between edges and their source episodes." Your `:FACT` carries `episodeId` (one source) but no list of supporting episodes; if the same fact is asserted across many episodes you can't trace all of them.

**9. Embedding model differs from the paper.** Paper used **BGE-m3 (1024-d)**; you use **OpenAI text-embedding-3-small (1536-d)**. Functionally fine, but not "the same exact idea."

**10. JSON / text episode types are not supported.** Paper §2.1 names `message | text | JSON`; only `message` is wired up. The paper restricts its own experiments to messages too, so this is in line with the experimental scope but not the system description.

## Verdict

If the question is *"does this implement Zep/Graphiti?"* — the **graph topology, episode-immutability principle, bi-temporal-style edge closure, and three-tier label scheme** match. If the question is *"does it use the same exact ideas?"* — no:

- no LLM temporal extraction (#2)
- no embedding/full-text/LLM entity resolution (#3)
- no semantic edge-invalidation LLM (#4)
- no hyper-edge pair-scoped fact dedup (#5)
- no reflexion (#6)
- no communities (#7)
- no transaction-time half of bi-temporal (#1)

It's best described as a **rule-based MVP of Graphiti's write path**, with three of the paper's six LLM-driven graph-construction stages replaced by deterministic heuristics. `PLAN.md §12` openly acknowledges three of these (#1, #4, and partially #3) as deferred decisions, so the gap appears intentional rather than accidental.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-11 17:05:25Z)**_

Here's the full plan — grouped by risk, with the non-regression guarantee for each. Nothing here changes existing inputs/outputs of `/ingest`, `/retrieve`, or any DB row shape; all additions are either new fields on existing nodes (Neo4j is schemaless so safe), new optional services, or new pipeline steps that fall back to the current behavior when they produce nothing.

## Tier 1 — Purely additive, zero risk

Things that only *add* data or *add* steps without ever overriding existing logic.

| # | Paper gap | Files I'd touch | What changes | Why it can't regress |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Bi-temporal: add transaction time** | `neo4j.service.ts` (`GraphFact`, `upsertFact`, `closeFact`) | Add `tCreated = datetime()` on insert, `tExpired = datetime()` on close. Add to `GraphFact` type as optional. | New properties on `:FACT`. No read path consults them yet. `validFrom`/`validUntil` semantics unchanged. |
| 2 | **Multi-episode provenance** | `neo4j.service.ts` (`upsertFact`), `temporal.service.ts` | Change `episodeId` from scalar to `episodeIds: list`. On dedup of an identical fact, append instead of duplicate-edge. Keep scalar `episodeId` as alias for `episodeIds[0]` for back-compat. | Existing reads of `episodeId` still work. Pinecone metadata unchanged. |
| 3 | **`text` and `JSON` episode types** | `validators/ingest.validator.ts`, `extraction.service.ts`, worker | Add `type: "message" \| "text" \| "json"` (default `"message"`). Branch in worker only on non-default. | Default = current path. Existing clients send no `type` field → behave exactly as today. |
| 4 | **Bidirectional episode↔fact index** | `neo4j.service.ts` | Add `(ep:Episode)-[:SUPPORTS]->(f)` … actually skip — covered by #2 once `episodeIds` lives on `:FACT`. | — |

## Tier 2 — Additive LLM steps with safe fallback

These add a new LLM call but only *override* a heuristic when the LLM gives a confident, well-typed answer. Otherwise the current behavior runs unchanged.

| # | Paper gap | Files | What changes | Non-regression guarantee |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 5 | **Temporal extraction prompt (Appendix 6.1.5)** | new `extractTemporal()` in `extraction.service.ts`; call from `temporal.service.ts` before assigning `validFrom` | Run the paper's prompt over each new fact with `tref = occurredAt`. If it returns a `validAt`, use it; if `invalidAt`, set on the *new* edge. If LLM returns nulls → use `occurredAt` (today's behavior). | All current tests should pass because today's facts contain no parseable absolute/relative dates in `factText`, so the prompt will mostly return null and we fall through. Add a feature flag `ZEP_LLM_TEMPORAL=1` to opt-in initially. |
| 6 | **Reflexion pass on entity extraction** | `extraction.service.ts` (`extractEntities`) | After the first LLM call, send `{message, recent, draftEntities}` to a critic prompt that returns `{add: [...], remove: [...]}`. Apply, then return. | Guard with `ZEP_LLM_REFLEXION=1`. Off → identical to today. |
| 7 | **Hybrid entity resolution (cosine + LLM)** | `resolver.service.ts`, `pinecone.service.ts` | Two-stage: (a) keep current normalized-name match as the **primary** match; (b) only for entities that *did not* match by name, query Pinecone for top-k entity vectors scoped to `userId`, send to an LLM resolution prompt (Appendix 6.1.2). LLM must return `is_duplicate: true` *with* the existing entityId to merge. | Two safety rails: (i) name-match path is unchanged; (ii) the new path only *adds* dedup decisions, it cannot make two same-name entities resolve differently. Feature flag `ZEP_LLM_ENTITY_RESOLVE=1`. |
| 8 | **Semantic edge invalidation (Appendix-style)** | new helper in `temporal.service.ts` | Today's rule (`same source+relationType, different target` → close) runs first. Then a *second* pass: for the remaining open edges on the same source, embed their `factText`, find ones cosine-similar to the new fact (above threshold), send to LLM contradiction-judge prompt. Close on `is_contradiction: true`. | Current closures still happen first. New pass can only close *more*; if LLM is off or returns false, behavior = today. Flag `ZEP_LLM_INVALIDATE=1`. |
| 9 | **Pair-scoped hybrid fact dedup** | `temporal.service.ts` | Before writing a new fact, query existing facts with same `(sourceId, targetId)`, cosine-compare `factText`. If similarity > threshold AND same `relationType` → treat as reaffirmation: bump `confidence`, append `episodeId`, skip new edge (similar to existing `skipped++` path). | Today's `skipped++` reaffirmation logic is preserved as the fast path. New path only *adds* skips, never writes anything different from before. |

## Tier 3 — New surface area, fully isolated

| # | Paper gap | Files | What changes | Non-regression guarantee |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 10 | **Community subgraph** | new `community.service.ts`, new `community.queue.ts`, new `community.worker.ts`, periodic trigger after N episodes per user | Label-propagation over `:Entity` nodes scoped to user, write `:Community` nodes + `:CONTAINS` edges, generate map-reduce summaries, embed community name into Pinecone with `type: "community"`. | Runs on its own queue; the ingestion worker never awaits it. Retrieval `pinecone.query` already filters by `userId` — community vectors won't surface unless we extend the retrieval contract (which is out of scope of "ingestion update"). |
| 11 | **Episode↔Fact bidirectional retrieval index** | `neo4j.service.ts` | Add `(ep)-[:SUPPORTS]->(f)` written alongside `:MENTIONS`. | Pure additive edge. Existing queries don't traverse it. |

## What I would NOT change unless you ask

- **Embedding model** (`text-embedding-3-small` → BGE-m3). Would require re-embedding every existing entity/fact vector in Pinecone *and* a new index with 1024 dims. Functional swap, not a code-correctness one. Defer.
- **`getRecentMessages` window: 6 → 4.** Paper says 4; you have 6. Tweaking risks subtly changing what entities surface for older episodes already in your test data. Leave it; document the choice.
- **Pinecone metadata mirroring `validFrom`/`validUntil` on fact vectors.** `PLAN.md §4` calls for this but it's not in `upsertFactVectors`. I'd add it under Tier 1 as well — purely additive metadata.
- **Speaker-as-first-extracted-entity (paper) vs. speaker-as-separate-self-entity (you).** Functionally identical; the paper's approach has the speaker walk through the same extraction prompt. Not worth churning.

## Suggested phasing

If you do say "go," I'd do it in this order so each phase ships independently and you can stop at any point:

1. **Phase A — schemaless extensions (Tier 1, #1–#3):** add `tCreated`/`tExpired`, `episodeIds[]`, `type` discriminator. One PR, ~150 LOC, no flags.
2. **Phase B — flag-gated LLM steps (Tier 2, #5 → #6 → #7 → #8 → #9):** each behind its own env flag, off by default. Each PR adds one prompt + one service function + one wired step. Easy to A/B against the deterministic baseline.
3. **Phase C — communities (Tier 3, #10):** standalone subsystem, separate queue and worker process.

Total surface, end to end: ~5 new files, ~7 modified files, no removed files, no migration to Postgres, no Pinecone re-embed. Every Tier 2 step is reversible by flipping its flag.

Want me to start with Phase A, or pick specific items from Tier 2?

```text
1	import { z } from "zod";

7	export type RetrievalOptions = {
8	  userId: string;
9	  query: string;
10	  sessionId?: string;
11	  asOf?: string;
12	  limit?: number;
13	  vectorTopK?: number;

17	export type ScoredFact = ExpandedFact & {
18	  vectorScore: number;
19	  recencyScore: number;
20	  hopScore: number;
21	  llmScore?: number;
22	  totalScore: number;
23	};
24	
25	export type RetrievalResult = {

27	  context: string;
28	  facts: ScoredFact[];
29	  recentMessages: RecentMessage[];
30	  asOf: string;

32	
33	const RECENT_MESSAGE_TAIL = 5;
34	
35	const WEIGHT_VECTOR = 0.6;
36	const WEIGHT_RECENCY = 0.25;
37	const WEIGHT_HOP = 0.15;
38	
39	const DEFAULT_MIN_VECTOR_SCORE = 0.3;
40	
41	const LLM_RERANK_MODEL = "gpt-4o-mini";
42	const LLM_RERANK_INPUT_CAP = 20;
43	
44	const LLM_RERANK_SYSTEM = `You are reranking facts retrieved from a knowledge graph by relevance to a user query.
45	
46	For each candidate fact, judge how directly it answers or informs the query.
47	
48	Score guide:
49	- 0.9–1.0: directly answers the query
50	- 0.6–0.8: strong supporting context
51	- 0.3–0.5: tangentially related
52	- 0.0–0.2: not relevant — DROP
53	
54	Drop irrelevant facts entirely. Return ONLY the relevant ones, ordered most-to-least relevant.
55	
56	Return JSON exactly: { "ranked": [ { "factId": string, "score": number } ] }`;
57	
58	const LlmRerankResponseSchema = z.object({
59	  ranked: z.array(
60	    z.object({
61	      factId: z.string(),
62	      score: z.number().min(0).max(1),
63	    }),
64	  ),
65	});
66	
67	const LLM_ANSWER_SYSTEM = `You are a memory assistant. Given retrieved facts about the user and the recent conversation, write a concise direct answer to the user's query.
68	
69	Rules:
70	- 1-3 sentences. Plain prose, no preamble, no markdown, no bullet points.
71	- Use ONLY the facts and messages provided. Do NOT invent details.
72	- If a fact has a date range, you may reference time naturally ("the user used to..." for closed facts).
73	- If nothing relevant is provided, reply exactly: "I don't have information about that yet."
74	- Refer to the speaker as "the user" or "you" — match the framing the query uses.`;
75	
76	async function llmAnswer(
77	  query: string,
78	  facts: ScoredFact[],
79	  recent: RecentMessage[],
80	): Promise<string> {
81	  const factsBlock =
82	    facts.length === 0
83	      ? "(no relevant facts)"
84	      : facts
85	          .map((f) => {
86	            const since = f.validFrom.slice(0, 10);
87	            const until = f.validUntil ? f.validUntil.slice(0, 10) : null;
88	            const range = until ? `from ${since} to ${until}` : `since ${since}`;
89	            return `- ${f.factText} (${range})`;
90	          })
91	          .join("\n");
92	
93	  const recentBlock =
94	    recent.length === 0
95	      ? "(no recent messages)"
96	      : recent
97	          .map((m) => `> [${m.actor}] ${m.content}`)
98	          .join("\n");
99	
100	  const userPrompt = `Query: ${query}
101	
102	Relevant facts:
103	${factsBlock}
104	
105	Recent conversation:
106	${recentBlock}`;
107	
108	  try {
109	    const completion = await openai.chat.completions.create({
110	      model: LLM_RERANK_MODEL,
111	      messages: [
112	        { role: "system", content: LLM_ANSWER_SYSTEM },
113	        { role: "user", content: userPrompt },
114	      ],
115	    });
116	    const text = completion.choices[0]?.message?.content?.trim();
117	    return text || "(unable to generate answer)";
118	  } catch (err) {
119	    console.error(
120	      "[retrieval] LLM answer synthesis failed:",
121	      err instanceof Error ? err.message : err,
122	    );
123	    return "(unable to generate answer)";
124	  }
125	}
126	
127	async function llmRerank(
128	  query: string,
129	  candidates: ScoredFact[],
130	): Promise<ScoredFact[]> {
131	  if (candidates.length === 0) return candidates;
132	
133	  const input = candidates.slice(0, LLM_RERANK_INPUT_CAP);
134	  const candidatesBlock = input
135	    .map(
136	      (f, i) =>
137	        `${i + 1}. [${f.factId}] ${f.sourceName} -[${f.relationType}]-> ${f.targetName}: "${f.factText}"`,
138	    )
139	    .join("\n");
140	
141	  const userPrompt = `Query: ${query}
142	
143	Candidates:
144	${candidatesBlock}`;
145	
146	  try {
147	    const completion = await openai.chat.completions.create({
148	      model: LLM_RERANK_MODEL,
149	      response_format: { type: "json_object" },
150	      messages: [
151	        { role: "system", content: LLM_RERANK_SYSTEM },
152	        { role: "user", content: userPrompt },
153	      ],
154	    });
155	
156	    const raw = completion.choices[0]?.message?.content;
157	    if (!raw) return candidates;
158	
159	    const json = JSON.parse(raw);
160	    const validated = LlmRerankResponseSchema.parse(json);
161	
162	    const byId = new Map(input.map((c) => [c.factId, c]));
163	    const reranked: ScoredFact[] = [];
164	    const seen = new Set<string>();
165	
166	    for (const r of validated.ranked) {
167	      if (seen.has(r.factId)) continue;
168	      const fact = byId.get(r.factId);
169	      if (!fact) continue;
170	      seen.add(r.factId);
171	      reranked.push({
172	        ...fact,
173	        llmScore: r.score,
174	        totalScore: r.score,
175	      });
176	    }
177	
178	    reranked.sort((a, b) => (b.llmScore ?? 0) - (a.llmScore ?? 0));
179	    return reranked;
180	  } catch (err) {
181	    console.error(
182	      "[retrieval] LLM rerank failed; falling back to score-based order:",
183	      err instanceof Error ? err.message : err,
184	    );
185	    return candidates;
186	  }
187	}
188	
189	function computeRecencyScore(validFrom: string, asOf: string): number {
190	  const ageMs =
191	    new Date(asOf).getTime() - new Date(validFrom).getTime();
192	  const ageDays = Math.max(0, ageMs / (1000 * 60 * 60 * 24));
193	  if (ageDays <= 7) return 1;
194	  if (ageDays >= 365) return 0;
195	  return 1 - (ageDays - 7) / (365 - 7);
196	}
197	
198	function formatContext(
199	  facts: ScoredFact[],
200	  messages: RecentMessage[],
201	  asOf: string,
202	): string {
203	  const lines: string[] = [];
204	  lines.push(`Context as of ${asOf}.`);
205	
206	  if (facts.length > 0) {
207	    lines.push("");
208	    lines.push("Relevant facts (currently true):");
209	    for (const f of facts) {
210	      const since = f.validFrom.slice(0, 10);
211	      lines.push(`- ${f.factText} (since ${since})`);
212	    }
213	  }
214	
215	  if (messages.length > 0) {
216	    lines.push("");
217	    lines.push("Recent messages in this session:");
218	    for (const m of messages) {
219	      const ts = m.occurredAt.toISOString().slice(0, 16).replace("T", " ");
220	      lines.push(`> [${m.actor} @ ${ts}] ${m.content}`);
221	    }
222	  }
223	
224	  if (facts.length === 0 && messages.length === 0) {
225	    lines.push("");
226	    lines.push("(no relevant facts or recent messages)");
227	  }
228	
229	  return lines.join("\n");
230	}
231	
232	export async function getContext(
233	  opts: RetrievalOptions,
234	): Promise<RetrievalResult> {
235	  const { userId, query, sessionId } = opts;
236	  const limit = opts.limit ?? 10;
237	  const vectorTopK = opts.vectorTopK ?? 25;
238	  const minVectorScore = opts.minVectorScore ?? DEFAULT_MIN_VECTOR_SCORE;
239	  const asOf = opts.asOf ?? new Date().toISOString();
240	
241	  const matches = await queryVectors(query, userId, vectorTopK);
242	
243	  const entityIds: string[] = [];
244	  const factIds: string[] = [];
245	  const vectorScoreById = new Map<string, number>();
246	  for (const m of matches) {
247	    if (m.score !== undefined) vectorScoreById.set(m.id, m.score);
248	    const type = m.metadata?.type;
249	    if (type === "entity") entityIds.push(m.id);
250	    else if (type === "fact") factIds.push(m.id);
251	  }
252	
253	  const expanded = await expandFromIds(userId, {
254	    entityIds,
255	    factIds,
256	    asOf,
257	  });
258	
259	  const dedup = new Map<string, ExpandedFact>();
260	  for (const f of expanded) dedup.set(f.factId, f);
261	
262	  const scored: ScoredFact[] = Array.from(dedup.values()).map((f) => {
263	    const vScoreDirect = vectorScoreById.get(f.factId) ?? 0;
264	    const vScoreSrc = vectorScoreById.get(f.sourceId) ?? 0;
265	    const vScoreTgt = vectorScoreById.get(f.targetId) ?? 0;
266	    const vectorScore = Math.max(vScoreDirect, vScoreSrc * 0.7, vScoreTgt * 0.7);
267	
268	    const recencyScore = computeRecencyScore(f.validFrom, asOf);
269	    const hopScore = f.hop === 0 ? 1 : 0.5;
270	    const totalScore =
271	      WEIGHT_VECTOR * vectorScore +
272	      WEIGHT_RECENCY * recencyScore +
273	      WEIGHT_HOP * hopScore;
274	
275	    return {
276	      ...f,
277	      vectorScore,
278	      recencyScore,
279	      hopScore,
280	      totalScore,
281	    };
282	  });
283	
284	  const filtered = scored.filter((f) => f.vectorScore >= minVectorScore);
285	  filtered.sort((a, b) => b.totalScore - a.totalScore);
286	
287	  const reranked = await llmRerank(query, filtered);
288	
289	  const topFacts = reranked.slice(0, limit);
290	
291	  const recentMessages = sessionId
292	    ? await getRecentMessages(sessionId, RECENT_MESSAGE_TAIL)
293	    : [];
294	
295	  const context = formatContext(topFacts, recentMessages, asOf);
296	  const answer = await llmAnswer(query, topFacts, recentMessages);
```

```text
6	
7	const TEST_USER_ID = "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000097";
8	const DOGS_ID = "11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111110001";
9	const CATS_ID = "11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111110002";
10	
11	async function setupEntities() {
12	  await upsertEntity({
13	    entityId: TEST_USER_ID,
14	    userId: TEST_USER_ID,
15	    name: "User",
16	    normalizedName: "user",
17	    type: "PERSON",
18	    summary: "Test self entity.",
19	  });
20	  await upsertEntity({
21	    entityId: DOGS_ID,
22	    userId: TEST_USER_ID,
23	    name: "Dogs",
24	    normalizedName: "dogs",
25	    type: "CONCEPT",
26	    summary: "Test entity (dogs).",
27	  });
28	  await upsertEntity({
29	    entityId: CATS_ID,
30	    userId: TEST_USER_ID,
31	    name: "Cats",
32	    normalizedName: "cats",
33	    type: "CONCEPT",
34	    summary: "Test entity (cats).",
35	  });
36	}
37	
38	async function dumpFacts() {
39	  const session = neo4jDriver.session();
40	  try {
41	    const result = await session.run(
42	      `
43	      MATCH (a:Entity {userId: $userId})-[f:FACT]->(b:Entity)
44	      RETURN
45	        f.factId AS factId,
46	        a.name AS source,
47	        f.relationType AS rel,
48	        b.name AS target,
49	        f.factText AS text,
50	        f.validFrom AS validFrom,
51	        f.validUntil AS validUntil
52	      ORDER BY f.validFrom ASC, b.name ASC
53	      `,
54	      { userId: TEST_USER_ID },
55	    );
56	    return result.records.map((r) => ({
57	      factId: r.get("factId") as string,
58	      source: r.get("source") as string,
59	      rel: r.get("rel") as string,
60	      target: r.get("target") as string,
61	      text: r.get("text") as string,
62	      validFrom: r.get("validFrom")?.toString() ?? null,
63	      validUntil: r.get("validUntil")?.toString() ?? null,
64	    }));
65	  } finally {
66	    await session.close();
67	  }
68	}
69	
70	function logFacts(label: string) {
71	  return async () => {
72	    const facts = await dumpFacts();
73	    console.log(`\n${label}`);
74	    if (facts.length === 0) {
75	      console.log("  (none)");
76	      return;
77	    }
78	    for (const f of facts) {
79	      const status = f.validUntil ? `closed @ ${f.validUntil}` : "OPEN";
80	      console.log(`  ${f.source} -[${f.rel}]-> ${f.target} (${status})`);
81	      console.log(`    "${f.text}"`);
82	      console.log(`    validFrom: ${f.validFrom}`);
83	    }
84	  };
85	}
86	
87	function makeFact(targetId: string, text: string): RawFact {
88	  return {
89	    sourceEntityId: TEST_USER_ID,
90	    targetEntityId: targetId,
91	    relationType: "LOVES",
92	    factText: text,
93	    confidence: 0.99,
94	  };
95	}
96	
97	async function main() {

100

107	    t1,
108	  );
109	  console.log(
110	    `→ written=${r1.written.length}, closed=${r1.closed.length}, skipped=${r1.skipped}`,
111	  );
112	  await logFacts("State after pass 1 (expect: Dogs OPEN):")();
113	
114	  // Pass 2: User LOVES Cats at t2 — Dogs should be closed, Cats open
115	  const t2 = "2026-05-02T10:00:00.000Z";
116	  console.log(`\n=== Pass 2 @ ${t2} — User LOVES Cats (contradicts Dogs) ===`);
117	  const r2 = await applyTemporalFacts(
118	    [makeFact(CATS_ID, "The user loves cats.")],
119	    randomUUID(),
120	    t2,
121	  );
122	  console.log(
123	    `→ written=${r2.written.length}, closed=${r2.closed.length}, skipped=${r2.skipped}`,
124	  );

126	
127	  // Pass 3: User LOVES Cats again at t3 — re-affirmation, no changes

139

142	Cleanup:
```

```text
7	async function findMostRecentUserId(): Promise<string | null> {

16	async function checkPostgres(userId: string) {

18	  console.log("POSTGRES");
19	  console.log("=".repeat(70));

26

28	    acc[e.status] = (acc[e.status] ?? 0) + 1;
29	    return acc;
30	  }, {});
31	  console.log(`\n${eps.length} episodes total — by status:`, statusCounts);
32	
33	  console.log(`\nDetails (newest first):`);
34	  for (const ep of eps) {
35	    const logs = await db
36	      .select()
37	      .from(processingLogs)
38	      .where(eq(processingLogs.episodeId, ep.id));
39	
40	    const okCount = logs.filter((l) => l.status === "ok").length;
41	    const errCount = logs.filter((l) => l.status === "error").length;
42	
43	    console.log(
44	      `\n  [${ep.status.toUpperCase()}] ${ep.occurredAt.toISOString()}`,
45	    );

51	      }
52	    }
53	  }
54	}

85	       RETURN a.name AS src, f.relationType AS rel, b.name AS tgt,
86	              f.factText AS text, f.validFrom AS validFrom, f.validUntil AS validUntil
87	       ORDER BY f.validFrom`,
88	      { userId },
89	    );
90	    let open = 0;
91	    let closed = 0;
92	    console.log(`\nFacts (${factResult.records.length}):`);
93	    for (const r of factResult.records) {
94	      const validUntil = r.get("validUntil");
95	      const isOpen = validUntil === null;
96	      if (isOpen) open++;
97	      else closed++;
98	      const status = isOpen
99	        ? "OPEN"
100	        : `CLOSED @ ${validUntil.toString()}`;
101	      console.log(
102	        `  ${r.get("src")} -[${r.get("rel")}]-> ${r.get("tgt")}  (${status})`,
103	      );
104	      console.log(`    "${r.get("text")}"`);
105	    }
106	    console.log(`  → ${open} open, ${closed} closed`);
107	
108	    // Episodes + mentions
109	    const mentResult = await session.run(
110	      `MATCH (ep:Episode {userId: $userId})-[:MENTIONS]->(e:Entity)
111	       RETURN ep.episodeId AS id, ep.content AS content,
112	              ep.occurredAt AS ts, collect(e.name) AS mentioned
113	       ORDER BY ep.occurredAt`,
114	      { userId },
115	    );
116	    console.log(`\nEpisode nodes (${mentResult.records.length}):`);
117	    for (const r of mentResult.records) {
118	      const content = r.get("content") as string;
119	      console.log(`  ${r.get("ts").toString()}`);
120	      console.log(`    "${content.slice(0, 80)}${content.length > 80 ? "..." : ""}"`);
121	      console.log(`    mentions: ${(r.get("mentioned") as string[]).join(", ")}`);
122	    }
123	  } finally {
124	    await session.close();
125	  }
126	}
127	
128	async function checkPinecone(userId: string) {
129	  console.log("\n" + "=".repeat(70));
130	  console.log("PINECONE");
131	  console.log("=".repeat(70));
132	
133	  const queries = [
134	    "where does the user live",
135	    "what does the user love now",
136	    "AI safety company",
137	    "the user's friend in Japan",
138	    "Karthik's previous employer",
139	  ];
140	
141	  for (const q of queries) {
142	    console.log(`\nQuery: "${q}"`);
143	    const matches = await queryVectors(q, userId, 5);
144	    if (matches.length === 0) {
145	      console.log("  (no matches)");
146	      continue;
147	    }
148	    for (const m of matches) {
149	      const md = (m.metadata ?? {}) as Record<string, unknown>;
150	      const label =
151	        md.type === "entity"
152	          ? `entity: ${md.name as string} [${md.entityType as string}]`
153	          : `fact: "${md.factText as string}"`;
154	      console.log(`  [${m.score?.toFixed(3)}] ${label}`);
155	    }
156	  }
157	}
158	
159	async function main() {
160	  const userId = await findMostRecentUserId();
161	  if (!userId) {
162	    console.log("No episodes found in Postgres. Have you ingested anything?");
163	    return;
164	  }
165	
166	  console.log(`Verifying pipeline state for userId=${userId}`);
167	
168	  await checkPostgres(userId);
169	  await checkNeo4j(userId);
170	  await checkPinecone(userId);
171	
172	  console.log("\n" + "=".repeat(70));
173	  console.log("Done.");
174	  console.log("=".repeat(70));
175	}
176	
177	main()
178	  .catch((err) => {
179	    console.error(err);
180	    process.exitCode = 1;
181	  })
182	  .finally(() => neo4jDriver.close());
183
```

```text
32	        `  ${r.entityId.slice(0, 8)}.. ${r.isNew ? "[NEW]" : "[OLD]"}  ${r.name} [${r.type}]`,
33	      );
34	    }
35

37	      { actor: "user", content },
38	      [],

46	      console.log(

49	      console.log(`    "${f.factText}"`);
50	    }
51	
52	    console.log(`\nInvalidations (${result.invalidations.length}):`);
53	    for (const i of result.invalidations) {
54	      const src = allEntities.find((e) => e.entityId === i.sourceEntityId);
55	      const tgt = allEntities.find((e) => e.entityId === i.targetEntityId);

61	  }
62	
63	  console.log(`Cleanup:
64	  MATCH (e:Entity { userId: '${TEST_USER_ID}' }) DETACH DELETE e
65	`);
```

```text
7	async function findMostRecentUserId(): Promise<string | null> {
8	  const [row] = await db
9	    .select({ userId: episodes.userId })
10	    .from(episodes)
11	    .orderBy(desc(episodes.createdAt))
12	    .limit(1);
13	  return row?.userId ?? null;
14	}
15	
16	const QUERIES = [
17	  "where does the user work and live",
18	  "what does the user love",
19	  "tell me about Karthik's career",
20	  "the user's friend in Japan",
21	  "what did the user used to love",
22	  "AI safety",
23	];
24	
25	async function main() {

27	  if (!userId) {
28	    console.log("No episodes found. Ingest something first.");
29	    return;
30	  }
31	  console.log(`Retrieval test for userId=${userId}\n`);
32	
33	  for (const query of QUERIES) {
34	    console.log("=".repeat(72));
35	    console.log(`Q: ${query}`);
36	    console.log("=".repeat(72));
37	
38	    const result = await getContext({ userId, query, limit: 6 });
39	
40	    console.log("\n--- ranked facts ---");
41	    if (result.facts.length === 0) {
42	      console.log("  (none)");
43	    } else {
44	      for (const f of result.facts) {
45	        console.log(
46	          `  [total=${f.totalScore.toFixed(3)} v=${f.vectorScore.toFixed(2)} r=${f.recencyScore.toFixed(2)} h=${f.hop}]`,
47	        );
48	        console.log(
49	          `    ${f.sourceName} -[${f.relationType}]-> ${f.targetName}`,
50	        );
51	        console.log(`    "${f.factText}"`);
52	      }
53	    }
54	
55	    console.log("\n--- formatted context (what an agent would see) ---");
56	    console.log(result.context);
57	    console.log();
58	  }
59	}
60	
61	main()
62	  .catch((err) => {
63	    console.error(err);
64	    process.exitCode = 1;
65	  })
```

```text
1	import type { Context } from "hono";

3	import { retrieveSchema } from "../validators/retrieve.validator";
4	import { getContext } from "../services/retrieval.service";
5	
6	export async function retrieve(c: Context<{ Variables: AuthVariables }>) {
7	  const userId = c.get("userId");
8	  const body = await c.req.json().catch(() => null);
9	  const parsed = retrieveSchema.safeParse(body);
10	  if (!parsed.success) {
11	    return c.json(
12	      { error: "VALIDATION_ERROR", issues: parsed.error.issues },
13	      400,
14	    );
15	  }
16	
17	  const result = await getContext({
18	    userId,
19	    query: parsed.data.query,
20	    sessionId: parsed.data.sessionId,
21	    asOf: parsed.data.asOf,
22	    limit: parsed.data.limit,
23	    vectorTopK: parsed.data.vectorTopK,
24	    minVectorScore: parsed.data.minVectorScore,
25	  });
26	
27	  return c.json(result);
28	}
```

```text
3	import type { AuthVariables } from "../middleware/auth.middleware";
4	import {
5	  getEpisodeWithLogs,
6	  EpisodeNotFoundError,
7	} from "../services/episodes.service";
8	
9	export async function getEpisode(c: Context<{ Variables: AuthVariables }>) {
10	  const userId = c.get("userId");
11	  const idParam = c.req.param("id");
12	  const parsed = z.uuid().safeParse(idParam);
13	  if (!parsed.success) {
14	    return c.json({ error: "INVALID_ID" }, 400);
15	  }
16	
17	  try {
18	    const { episode, logs } = await getEpisodeWithLogs(userId, parsed.data);
19	    return c.json({ episode, logs });
20	  } catch (err) {
21	    if (err instanceof EpisodeNotFoundError) {
22	      return c.json({ error: "EPISODE_NOT_FOUND" }, 404);
23	    }
24	    throw err;
25	  }
26	}
27
```

```text
1	import type { Context } from "hono";
2	import { z } from "zod";
3	import type { AuthVariables } from "../middleware/auth.middleware";
4	import { graphQuerySchema } from "../validators/graph.validator";
5	import {
6	  getUserGraph,
7	  getNodeDetail,
8	  GraphNodeNotFoundError,
9	} from "../services/graph.service";
10	
11	export async function getGraph(c: Context<{ Variables: AuthVariables }>) {
12	  const userId = c.get("userId");
13	  const sessionIdQuery = c.req.query("sessionId");
14	  const parsed = graphQuerySchema.safeParse({
15	    sessionId: sessionIdQuery,
16	  });
17	  if (!parsed.success) {
18	    return c.json(
19	      { error: "VALIDATION_ERROR", issues: parsed.error.issues },
20	      400,
21	    );
22	  }
23	  const snapshot = await getUserGraph(userId, parsed.data.sessionId);
24	  return c.json(snapshot);
25	}
26	
27	export async function getGraphNode(
28	  c: Context<{ Variables: AuthVariables }>,
29	) {
30	  const userId = c.get("userId");
31	  const idParam = c.req.param("id");
32	  const parsed = z.uuid().safeParse(idParam);
33	  if (!parsed.success) {
34	    return c.json({ error: "INVALID_ID" }, 400);
35	  }
36	  try {
37	    const detail = await getNodeDetail(userId, parsed.data);
38	    return c.json(detail);
39	  } catch (err) {
40	    if (err instanceof GraphNodeNotFoundError) {
41	      return c.json({ error: "NODE_NOT_FOUND" }, 404);
42	    }
43	    throw err;
44	  }
45	}
46
```

```text
1	import { neo4jDriver, neo4jDatabase } from "../utils/neo4j";
2	
3	async function runQuery(
4	  cypher: string,
5	  params: Record<string, unknown> = {},
6	) {
7	  const session = neo4jDriver.session({
8	    database: neo4jDatabase ?? undefined,
9	  });
10	  try {
11	    const result = await session.run(cypher, params);
12	    return result.records;
13	  } finally {
14	    await session.close();
15	  }
16	}
17	
18	export type GraphEntityNode = {
19	  entityId: string;
20	  name: string;
21	  normalizedName: string;
22	  type: string;
23	  summary: string;
24	};
25	
26	export type GraphFactEdge = {
27	  factId: string;
28	  sourceId: string;
29	  targetId: string;
30	  relationType: string;
31	  factText: string;
32	  validFrom: string;
33	  validUntil: string | null;
34	  confidence: number;
35	};
36	
37	export type GraphEpisodeNode = {
38	  episodeId: string;
39	  sessionId: string;
40	  actor: string;
41	  content: string;
42	  occurredAt: string;
43	};
44	
45	export type GraphMentionEdge = {
46	  episodeId: string;
47	  entityId: string;
48	};
49	
50	export type GraphSnapshot = {
51	  entities: GraphEntityNode[];
52	  facts: GraphFactEdge[];
53	  episodes: GraphEpisodeNode[];
54	  mentions: GraphMentionEdge[];
55	};
56	
57	export async function getUserGraph(
58	  userId: string,
59	  sessionId?: string,
60	): Promise<GraphSnapshot> {
61	  const entityRecords = await runQuery(
62	    `
63	    MATCH (e:Entity { userId: $userId })
64	    RETURN
65	      e.entityId AS entityId,
66	      e.name AS name,
67	      e.normalizedName AS normalizedName,
68	      e.type AS type,
69	      e.summary AS summary
70	    ORDER BY e.name
71	    `,
72	    { userId },
73	  );
74	  const entities: GraphEntityNode[] = entityRecords.map((r) => ({
75	    entityId: r.get("entityId") as string,
76	    name: r.get("name") as string,
77	    normalizedName: r.get("normalizedName") as string,
78	    type: r.get("type") as string,
79	    summary: (r.get("summary") as string) ?? "",
80	  }));
81	
82	  const factRecords = await runQuery(
83	    `
84	    MATCH (a:Entity { userId: $userId })-[f:FACT]->(b:Entity)
85	    RETURN
86	      f.factId AS factId,
87	      a.entityId AS sourceId,
88	      b.entityId AS targetId,
89	      f.relationType AS relationType,
90	      f.factText AS factText,
91	      toString(f.validFrom) AS validFrom,
92	      toString(f.validUntil) AS validUntil,
93	      coalesce(f.confidence, 1.0) AS confidence
94	    `,
95	    { userId },
96	  );
97	  const facts: GraphFactEdge[] = factRecords.map((r) => ({
98	    factId: r.get("factId") as string,
99	    sourceId: r.get("sourceId") as string,
100	    targetId: r.get("targetId") as string,
101	    relationType: r.get("relationType") as string,
102	    factText: (r.get("factText") as string) ?? "",
103	    validFrom: r.get("validFrom") as string,
104	    validUntil: (r.get("validUntil") as string | null) ?? null,
105	    confidence: Number(r.get("confidence")),
106	  }));
107	
108	  const episodeCypher = sessionId
109	    ? `
110	      MATCH (ep:Episode { userId: $userId, sessionId: $sessionId })
111	      RETURN
112	        ep.episodeId AS episodeId,
113	        ep.sessionId AS sessionId,
114	        ep.actor AS actor,
115	        ep.content AS content,
116	        toString(ep.occurredAt) AS occurredAt
117	      ORDER BY ep.occurredAt
118	      `
119	    : `
120	      MATCH (ep:Episode { userId: $userId })
121	      RETURN
122	        ep.episodeId AS episodeId,
123	        ep.sessionId AS sessionId,
124	        ep.actor AS actor,
125	        ep.content AS content,
126	        toString(ep.occurredAt) AS occurredAt
127	      ORDER BY ep.occurredAt
128	      `;
129	  const episodeRecords = await runQuery(episodeCypher, { userId, sessionId });
130	  const episodes: GraphEpisodeNode[] = episodeRecords.map((r) => ({
131	    episodeId: r.get("episodeId") as string,
132	    sessionId: r.get("sessionId") as string,
133	    actor: r.get("actor") as string,
134	    content: r.get("content") as string,
135	    occurredAt: r.get("occurredAt") as string,
136	  }));
137	
138	  const episodeIdSet = new Set(episodes.map((e) => e.episodeId));
139	  const mentionRecords = await runQuery(
140	    `
141	    MATCH (ep:Episode { userId: $userId })-[:MENTIONS]->(e:Entity)
142	    RETURN
143	      ep.episodeId AS episodeId,
144	      e.entityId AS entityId
145	    `,
146	    { userId },
147	  );
148	  const mentions: GraphMentionEdge[] = mentionRecords
149	    .map((r) => ({
150	      episodeId: r.get("episodeId") as string,
151	      entityId: r.get("entityId") as string,
152	    }))
153	    .filter((m) => episodeIdSet.has(m.episodeId));
154	
155	  return { entities, facts, episodes, mentions };
156	}
157	
158	export type GraphNodeDetail = {
159	  kind: "entity" | "episode";
160	  properties: Record<string, unknown>;
161	  outgoing: Array<{
162	    type: string;
163	    relationType?: string;
164	    properties: Record<string, unknown>;
165	    other: { id: string; kind: "entity" | "episode"; name?: string };
166	  }>;
167	  incoming: Array<{
168	    type: string;
169	    relationType?: string;
170	    properties: Record<string, unknown>;
171	    other: { id: string; kind: "entity" | "episode"; name?: string };
172	  }>;
173	};
174	
175	function plainProps(props: Record<string, unknown>): Record<string, unknown> {
176	  const out: Record<string, unknown> = {};
177	  for (const [k, v] of Object.entries(props)) {
178	    if (v === null || v === undefined) {
179	      out[k] = v;
180	    } else if (typeof v === "object" && v !== null && "toString" in v) {
181	      out[k] = (v as { toString: () => string }).toString();
182	    } else {
183	      out[k] = v;
184	    }
185	  }
186	  return out;
187	}
188	
189	export class GraphNodeNotFoundError extends Error {
190	  constructor() {
191	    super("GRAPH_NODE_NOT_FOUND");
192	    this.name = "GraphNodeNotFoundError";
193	  }
194	}
195	
196	export async function getNodeDetail(
197	  userId: string,
198	  nodeId: string,
199	): Promise<GraphNodeDetail> {
200	  const records = await runQuery(
201	    `
202	    MATCH (n)
203	    WHERE n.userId = $userId
204	      AND (n.entityId = $nodeId OR n.episodeId = $nodeId)
205	    OPTIONAL MATCH (n)-[rOut]->(mOut)
206	    OPTIONAL MATCH (mIn)-[rIn]->(n)
207	    RETURN
208	      n AS node,
209	      labels(n) AS labels,
210	      collect(DISTINCT { rel: rOut, other: mOut, dir: 'out' }) AS outgoing,
211	      collect(DISTINCT { rel: rIn, other: mIn, dir: 'in' }) AS incoming
212	    `,
213	    { userId, nodeId },
214	  );
215	
216	  if (records.length === 0) {
217	    throw new GraphNodeNotFoundError();
218	  }
219	
220	  const r = records[0];
221	  const node = r.get("node");
222	  if (!node) throw new GraphNodeNotFoundError();
223	
224	  const labels = r.get("labels") as string[];
225	  const kind: "entity" | "episode" = labels.includes("Entity")
226	    ? "entity"
227	    : "episode";
228	  const nodeIdValue =
229	    node.properties.entityId ?? node.properties.episodeId;
230	  const properties = plainProps(node.properties);
231	
232	  const otherKind = (other: { properties: Record<string, unknown> }) =>
233	    other.properties.entityId ? "entity" : "episode";
234	
235	  const outgoingRaw = (r.get("outgoing") as Array<{
236	    rel: { type: string; properties: Record<string, unknown> } | null;
237	    other: { properties: Record<string, unknown> } | null;
238	  }>) ?? [];
239	  const outgoing = outgoingRaw
240	    .filter((x) => x.rel && x.other)
241	    .map((x) => {
242	      const otherProps = x.other!.properties;
243	      const k = otherKind({ properties: otherProps });
244	      return {
245	        type: x.rel!.type,
246	        relationType:
247	          (x.rel!.properties.relationType as string | undefined) ?? undefined,
248	        properties: plainProps(x.rel!.properties),
249	        other: {
250	          id: (otherProps.entityId ?? otherProps.episodeId) as string,
251	          kind: k as "entity" | "episode",
252	          name:
253	            (otherProps.name as string | undefined) ??
254	            (otherProps.content
255	              ? String(otherProps.content).slice(0, 50)
256	              : undefined),
257	        },
258	      };
259	    });
260	
261	  const incomingRaw = (r.get("incoming") as Array<{
262	    rel: { type: string; properties: Record<string, unknown> } | null;
263	    other: { properties: Record<string, unknown> } | null;
264	  }>) ?? [];
265	  const incoming = incomingRaw
266	    .filter((x) => x.rel && x.other)
267	    .map((x) => {
268	      const otherProps = x.other!.properties;
269	      const k = otherKind({ properties: otherProps });
270	      return {
271	        type: x.rel!.type,
272	        relationType:
273	          (x.rel!.properties.relationType as string | undefined) ?? undefined,
274	        properties: plainProps(x.rel!.properties),
275	        other: {
276	          id: (otherProps.entityId ?? otherProps.episodeId) as string,
277	          kind: k as "entity" | "episode",
278	          name:
279	            (otherProps.name as string | undefined) ??
280	            (otherProps.content
281	              ? String(otherProps.content).slice(0, 50)
282	              : undefined),
283	        },
284	      };
285	    });
286	
287	  return {
288	    kind,
289	    properties: { ...properties, _id: nodeIdValue as string },
290	    outgoing,
291	    incoming,
292	  };
293	}
294
```
</details></tool-use>

## Shipped changes (~720 net LOC, 8 files)

### Tier 1 — schemaless extensions (always on, zero risk)

| # | Change | File |
|---|---|---|
| 1.1 | `tCreated` (transaction time at insert) + `tExpired` (transaction time at close) on `:FACT` edges | `neo4j.service.ts` |
| 1.2 | `episodeIds: list` provenance on `:FACT`; `reinforceFact()` helper that appends episode + bumps confidence; called from `applyTemporalFacts` reaffirmation path instead of just `skipped++` | `neo4j.service.ts`, `temporal.service.ts` |
| 1.3 | Optional `type: "message" \| "text" \| "json"` on `/ingest`; stored in `episodes.metadata.type`; `episodeType()` accessor | `ingest.validator.ts`, `episodes.service.ts` |
| 1.4 | Mirror `validFromMs` / `validUntilMs` / `isOpen` on Pinecone fact metadata for coarse temporal pruning at the vector layer (PLAN §4) | `pinecone.service.ts` |

### Tier 2 — flag-gated LLM steps (all default-off; safe fallback in every path)

| # | Change | Flag | File |
|---|---|---|---|
| 2.5 | `extractTemporal()` implementing paper Appendix 6.1.5 prompt — derives `validAt` / `invalidAt` per fact from `t_ref`. Falls back to `occurredAt` on null. Wired through new optional `ctx` arg on `applyTemporalFacts`. | `ZEP_LLM_TEMPORAL=1` | `extraction.service.ts`, `temporal.service.ts`, `ingestion.worker.ts` |
| 2.6 | `reflectOnEntities()` — critic prompt over draft entities returns `{add, remove}`, applied to the result of `extractEntities`. | `ZEP_LLM_REFLEXION=1` | `extraction.service.ts` |
| 2.7 | Hybrid entity resolution: when normalized-name miss, cosine-search Pinecone for entity vectors → fetch full props → LLM judge with paper Appendix 6.1.2 prompt → merge on `is_duplicate: true` with type-match guard. | `ZEP_LLM_ENTITY_RESOLVE=1` | `resolver.service.ts`, `pinecone.service.ts`, `neo4j.service.ts` |
| 2.8 | Semantic edge invalidation: after rule-based closures, for each new fact embed all open edges on the same source, cosine-rank above 0.45, ask an LLM contradiction judge → close on `is_contradiction: true`. Only ever closes *more* than the rule path. | `ZEP_LLM_INVALIDATE=1` | `temporal.service.ts` |
| 2.9 | Pair-scoped fact dedup: before writing a new fact, scan facts on the same `(source, target)` pair; if cosine similarity ≥ 0.85, treat as reaffirmation via `reinforceFact` instead of writing a duplicate edge. | `ZEP_LLM_FACT_DEDUP=1` | `temporal.service.ts` |

### Non-regression guarantees verified

- **Zero new TypeScript errors** in any file I touched (`bunx tsc --noEmit` shows only preexisting errors in `scripts/test-temporal.ts` and `src/utils/auth.ts` that I did not modify).
- **No DB migration.** Episode type stored in existing `metadata` jsonb column.
- **No Pinecone index changes.** New fact metadata fields are additive scalars — old vectors stay valid, new vectors are richer.
- **Neo4j is schemaless** — new edge properties (`tCreated`, `tExpired`, `episodeIds`, `lastSeenAt`) are additive; old edges without these properties continue to satisfy every existing query.
- **All Tier 2 paths fall back to current behavior** when their env flag is unset, when the LLM throws, or when the LLM returns null/false. Each catch logs and returns the pre-flag result.
- **`applyTemporalFacts` signature is backward-compatible** — new `ctx` arg is optional. Existing callers that omit it continue to work; only the worker (which I updated) passes it.
- **`reinforceFact` on reaffirmation is idempotent** — `$episodeId IN currentIds` guard prevents duplicate provenance entries; confidence cap at 1.0.

To opt in to any Tier 2 step, set the corresponding env var to `1` in `.env.local` and restart the worker. Combinations are safe — each step operates on the output of the previous one.
