Here are end-to-end architecture diagrams that match the current code exactly — including every Tier 1 addition and Tier 2 flag-gated branch.

## 1. Storage topology (where each piece of data lives)

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                              POSTGRES (Neon)                                 │
│  ┌────────┐  ┌──────────┐  ┌──────────────────────────┐  ┌────────────────┐  │
│  │ users  │  │ sessions │  │ episodes                 │  │processing_logs │  │
│  │  id    │  │  id      │  │  id, user_id, actor      │  │ episode_id     │  │
│  │ email  │  │ user_id  │  │  content, status         │  │ step, status   │  │
│  │ pwhash │  │ title    │  │  metadata.type ←Tier 1.3 │  │ duration_ms    │  │
│  └────────┘  └──────────┘  │  occurred_at, processed  │  └────────────────┘  │
│                            └──────────────────────────┘                      │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                              NEO4J (graph)                                   │
│                                                                              │
│   (:Episode)──[:MENTIONS]──▶(:Entity)──[:FACT]──▶(:Entity)                   │
│   ▲                          ▲                                               │
│   │ episodeId, content       │ entityId, name, normalizedName, type, summary │
│   │ actor, occurredAt        │                                               │
│   │ sessionId, userId        │ FACT props:                                   │
│                              │   factId, relationType, factText              │
│                              │   validFrom, validUntil    ← event time (T)   │
│                              │   tCreated, tExpired       ← txn time (T'), Tier 1.1
│                              │   episodeIds: [..]         ← Tier 1.2         │
│                              │   confidence, lastSeenAt                      │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                            PINECONE (index "zep")                            │
│                                                                              │
│  one index, type discriminator in metadata, always filter by userId          │
│                                                                              │
│  entity vector:   id=entityId  meta={ type:"entity", userId, name, ... }     │
│  fact vector:     id=factId    meta={ type:"fact",   userId, sourceId,       │
│                                       targetId, relationType, factText,      │
│                                       validFromMs, validUntilMs, isOpen      │
│                                       ← Tier 1.4 }                           │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                            REDIS / BullMQ                                    │
│   queue: "memory-ingestion"   job: { episodeId }   attempts: 3, expo backoff │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## 2. Ingestion pipeline

```
            ┌──────────────┐   POST /ingest                          ┌────────────┐
            │   Client     │  { sessionId, actor, content,           │  Auth MW   │
            │   + Bearer   │    occurredAt?, type? }                 │ verify JWT │
            │     JWT      │ ───────────────────────────────────────▶│ → userId   │
            └──────────────┘                                         └─────┬──────┘
                                                                           ▼
                                                          ┌────────────────────────┐
                                                          │ ingest.controller      │
                                                          │  zod-validate body     │
                                                          └───────────┬────────────┘
                                                                      ▼
                                                          ┌────────────────────────┐
                                                          │ episodes.service       │
                                                          │   .createEpisode()     │
                                                          │ 1. verify session∈user │
                                                          │ 2. INSERT episodes     │
                                                          │    metadata.type? ←1.3 │
                                                          │ 3. enqueue Bull job    │
                                                          └─┬────┬──────────────┬──┘
                                                            │    │              │
                                       202 {episodeId} ◀────┘    ▼              ▼
                                                       ┌──────────────┐  ┌──────────────┐
                                                       │  Postgres    │  │ Redis/BullMQ │
                                                       │  status=     │  │ enqueue      │
                                                       │  "queued"    │  │  episodeId   │
                                                       └──────────────┘  └──────┬───────┘
                                                                                │
─────────────────────────────────  process boundary  ──────────────────────────┼─────
                                                                                ▼
                                                       ┌──────────────────────────────┐
                                                       │ ingestion.worker             │
                                                       │ (bun, concurrency=4)         │
                                                       │ each step → processing_logs  │
                                                       └──────────────┬───────────────┘
                                                                      │
   ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────┐
   ▼                                                                  │                              ▼
┌────────────────┐   fetch_episode   ┌────────────────┐   fetch_recent (last 6 msgs)   ┌────────────────┐
│   Postgres     │ ◀──────────────── │     Worker     │ ─────────────────────────────▶ │    Postgres    │
└────────────────┘                   └───────┬────────┘                                 └────────────────┘
                                             │
                                             ▼  extract_entities
                                  ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
                                  │  OpenAI: entity-extraction prompt   │
                                  │  (gpt-4o-mini)                      │
                                  │   ┌──────────────────────────────┐  │
                                  │   │ [flag] ZEP_LLM_REFLEXION=1   │  │
                                  │   │   critic prompt → {add,rm}   │  │
                                  │   └──────────────────────────────┘  │
                                  └────────────────┬────────────────────┘
                                                   │  raw entities
                                                   ▼  resolve_entities
                                  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
                                  │ resolver.service.resolveEntities            │
                                  │  1. normalize names → exact match in Neo4j  │
                                  │  ┌────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
                                  │  │ [flag] ZEP_LLM_ENTITY_RESOLVE=1        │ │
                                  │  │  for misses: embed → cosine on         │ │
                                  │  │  Pinecone (entity, userId, type-match) │ │
                                  │  │  → LLM resolution prompt → merge       │ │
                                  │  └────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
                                  │  2. mint new UUIDs for unresolved           │
                                  │  3. upsertEntities() in Neo4j               │
                                  └────────────────┬────────────────────────────┘
                                                   ▼  resolved + self
                                                   │
                                                   ▼  extract_facts
                                  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
                                  │ OpenAI: fact-extraction prompt              │
                                  │  → { facts[], invalidations[] }             │
                                  │     facts:   (src,rel,tgt,text,conf)        │
                                  │     invals:  (src,rel,tgt)  explicit close  │
                                  └────────────────┬────────────────────────────┘
                                                   ▼  apply_temporal
                          ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
                          │ temporal.service.applyTemporalFacts                          │
                          │                                                              │
                          │  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐    │
                          │  │ [flag] ZEP_LLM_TEMPORAL=1                            │    │
                          │  │  for each fact: extractTemporal(text, ctx, t_ref)    │    │
                          │  │   → {validAt, invalidAt}  (paper App. 6.1.5)         │    │
                          │  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘    │
                          │                                                              │
                          │  A. rule-based: per (source, relationType) find open edges,  │
                          │     close any whose target ≠ new target  → closeFact         │
                          │                                                              │
                          │  B. write new facts (one per non-reaffirmation):             │
                          │     reaffirmation? → reinforceFact (episodeIds+=, conf++)    │
                          │     ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
                          │     │ [flag] ZEP_LLM_FACT_DEDUP=1                        │   │
                          │     │  same (src,tgt) cosine ≥ 0.85 → reinforceFact      │   │
                          │     └────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
                          │     else → upsertFact (sets tCreated, episodeIds=[ep])       │
                          │                                                              │
                          │  C. explicit invalidations from LLM → closeFact              │
                          │                                                              │
                          │  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐    │
                          │  │ [flag] ZEP_LLM_INVALIDATE=1                          │    │
                          │  │  for each new fact: embed open edges on same source, │    │
                          │  │  cosine ≥ 0.45 → LLM contradiction judge → closeFact │    │
                          │  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘    │
                          └────────────────┬────────────────────────────┬────────────────┘
                                           │                            │
                                           ▼  written facts             ▼  closes + reinforces
                          ┌──────────────────────────┐         ┌──────────────────────────┐
                          │ upsert_fact_vectors      │         │ Neo4j: upsertFact,       │
                          │  → Pinecone              │         │ closeFact, reinforceFact │
                          │  +validFromMs            │         │ (tExpired set on close)  │
                          │  +validUntilMs, isOpen   │         └──────────────────────────┘
                          └──────────────────────────┘
                                           │
                                           ▼  upsert_entity_vectors (new entities only)
                          ┌──────────────────────────┐
                          │  Pinecone entity vectors │
                          └──────────────────────────┘
                                           │
                                           ▼  create_episode_node + link_episode_entities
                          ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐
                          │  Neo4j:                                      │
                          │   MERGE (:Episode {episodeId})               │
                          │   FOREACH entity: MERGE                      │
                          │     (ep)-[:MENTIONS {timestamp}]->(ent)      │
                          └────────────────┬─────────────────────────────┘
                                           ▼
                          ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐
                          │  Postgres: status="processed", processed_at  │
                          └──────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## 3. Retrieval pipeline

```
            ┌──────────────┐   POST /retrieve                                ┌────────────┐
            │   Client     │  { query, sessionId?, asOf?, limit?,            │  Auth MW   │
            │   + JWT      │    vectorTopK?, minVectorScore? }               │ → userId   │
            └──────┬───────┘ ───────────────────────────────────────────────▶└─────┬──────┘
                   │                                                               ▼
                   │                                              ┌────────────────────────────┐
                   │                                              │ retrieve.controller        │
                   │                                              │  zod-validate              │
                   │                                              └─────────────┬──────────────┘
                   │                                                            ▼
                   │                                              ┌────────────────────────────┐
                   │                                              │ retrieval.service          │
                   │                                              │  .getContext(opts)         │
                   │                                              └─────┬──────────────────────┘
                   │                                                    ▼
                   │                                  ┌────────────────────────────────────┐
                   │                                  │ 1. embed(query)                    │
                   │                                  │    OpenAI text-embedding-3-small   │
                   │                                  └────────────────┬───────────────────┘
                   │                                                   ▼
                   │                                  ┌────────────────────────────────────┐
                   │                                  │ 2. Pinecone queryVectors           │
                   │                                  │    filter: { userId }              │
                   │                                  │    topK: vectorTopK (default 25)   │
                   │                                  │    returns matches w/ score + meta │
                   │                                  └────────────────┬───────────────────┘
                   │                                                   ▼
                   │              split by metadata.type                ┌──────────────┐
                   │            ┌────────────────────────────┐          │ entityIds[]  │
                   │            │  for m in matches:         │          │ factIds[]    │
                   │            │   if type=="entity"        │ ───────▶ │ scoreById{}  │
                   │            │     entityIds.push(m.id)   │          └──────┬───────┘
                   │            │   if type=="fact"          │                 │
                   │            │     factIds.push(m.id)     │                 │
                   │            │   scoreById[m.id]=m.score  │                 │
                   │            └────────────────────────────┘                 │
                   │                                                           ▼
                   │                                ┌──────────────────────────────────────┐
                   │                                │ 3. Neo4j expandFromIds(userId,       │
                   │                                │      entityIds, factIds, asOf)       │
                   │                                │                                      │
                   │                                │  MATCH (a:Entity)-[f:FACT]->(b)      │
                   │                                │  WHERE a.userId=$uid                 │
                   │                                │   AND ( f.factId IN $factIds         │
                   │                                │      OR a.entityId IN $entityIds     │
                   │                                │      OR b.entityId IN $entityIds )   │
                   │                                │   AND f.validFrom <= asOf            │
                   │                                │   AND coalesce(f.validUntil,'9999')  │
                   │                                │       >= asOf                        │
                   │                                │  hop=0 if direct match, else 1       │
                   │                                └──────────────────┬───────────────────┘
                   │                                                   ▼
                   │                ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
                   │                │ 4. score each ExpandedFact:                            │
                   │                │   vectorScore = max(score[factId],                     │
                   │                │                      0.7·score[srcId],                 │
                   │                │                      0.7·score[tgtId])                 │
                   │                │   recencyScore = 1 if ≤7d, 0 if ≥1y, linear between    │
                   │                │   hopScore     = 1 if hop=0 else 0.5                   │
                   │                │   total = 0.6·v + 0.25·r + 0.15·h                      │
                   │                │   filter vectorScore ≥ minVectorScore (default 0.3)    │
                   │                │   sort desc by total                                   │
                   │                └────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────┘
                   │                                         ▼
                   │                ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
                   │                │ 5. llmRerank (gpt-4o-mini, top 20)                     │
                   │                │    JSON: { ranked: [{factId, score}] }                 │
                   │                │    drops irrelevant; falls back to score order on err  │
                   │                └────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────┘
                   │                                         ▼
                   │                            slice top `limit` (default 10)
                   │                                         │
                   │       ┌─────────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────┐
                   │       ▼                                                            ▼
                   │  ┌─────────────────────────┐                          ┌──────────────────────────┐
                   │  │ 6. (if sessionId)       │                          │ 7. formatContext         │
                   │  │   getRecentMessages     │                          │  "Facts (currently true):│
                   │  │   last 5 from Postgres  │                          │   - {factText} (since X) │
                   │  │   for grounding         │                          │   Recent messages: ..."  │
                   │  └────────────┬────────────┘                          └────────────┬─────────────┘
                   │               │                                                    │
                   │               └────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────┘
                   │                                    ▼
                   │                ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
                   │                │ 8. llmAnswer (gpt-4o-mini)                             │
                   │                │    "given facts + recent, write 1-3 sentence answer"   │
                   │                └────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────┘
                   │                                         ▼
                   │                ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
                   └◀───────────────│  response:                                             │
                                    │   { answer, context, facts, recentMessages, asOf }     │
                                    └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## 4. Where the paper's pieces map

```
Paper concept (§2)                            Implementation
──────────────────────────────────────────    ─────────────────────────────────────────
Episode subgraph Ge                           :Episode nodes + :MENTIONS edges
  episodes are non-lossy                      Postgres episodes table + Neo4j :Episode
Semantic Entity subgraph Gs                   :Entity nodes + :FACT edges
Community subgraph Gc                         (NOT YET — Tier 3 deferred)
  resolved against existing graph entities    normalized-name match
                                              + [flag] cosine + LLM (paper App. 6.1.2)
hyper-edge / pair-scoped dedup                [flag] cosine on (src,tgt) ≥ 0.85
bi-temporal: t_valid / t_invalid (T)          validFrom / validUntil
bi-temporal: t'_created / t'_expired (T')     tCreated / tExpired                 ←Tier 1.1
multi-episode provenance                      episodeIds[] on :FACT               ←Tier 1.2
LLM temporal extraction (App. 6.1.5)          [flag] extractTemporal()            ←Tier 2.5
reflexion entity-extraction                   [flag] reflectOnEntities()          ←Tier 2.6
LLM-semantic edge invalidation                [flag] semanticInvalidate()         ←Tier 2.8
embed entity name (1024-d, BGE-m3)            embed(name+summary), text-embedding-3-small (1536-d)
search: cosine + BM25 + BFS                   cosine only (queryVectors)
reranker: RRF / MMR / cross-encoder           weighted blend + LLM cross-encoder
context constructor χ                         formatContext() + llmAnswer()
```

Flags in `.env.local` (all default off):

```
ZEP_LLM_TEMPORAL=1         # 2.5  per-fact valid_at/invalid_at extraction
ZEP_LLM_REFLEXION=1        # 2.6  entity-extraction critic pass
ZEP_LLM_ENTITY_RESOLVE=1   # 2.7  cosine + LLM entity dedup on name misses
ZEP_LLM_INVALIDATE=1       # 2.8  semantic contradiction detection
ZEP_LLM_FACT_DEDUP=1       # 2.9  pair-scoped semantic fact dedup
```

Each flag is independent; enabling any subset only adds work — every path falls back to the rule-based behavior if the LLM call errors or returns null/false.

could you explain the ingestion pipeline end to end full thoroughly and tell me what changes did you add exactly and how it improvd.changed the ingestion

---

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-11 17:39:10Z)**_

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-11 17:40:39Z)**_

# End-to-end ingestion pipeline

Following one message from the moment it hits `POST /ingest` to the moment its derived facts are queryable. At each phase I flag exactly what changed and why.

---

## Phase 1 — Request acceptance (synchronous, ~5 ms)

**File:** `controllers/ingest.controller.ts` → `validators/ingest.validator.ts` → `services/episodes.service.ts`

The client POSTs:
```json
{ "sessionId": "...", "actor": "user", "content": "...",
  "occurredAt": "2026-05-11T...", "type": "message" }
```
1. Auth middleware verifies the JWT and sets `userId` on the request context.
2. `ingest.controller` parses the body with `ingestSchema` (zod).
3. `episodes.service.createEpisode()`:
   - SELECT to confirm the session belongs to this user (cross-tenant guard).
   - INSERT a row into `episodes` with `status="queued"`, `occurred_at`, `actor`, `content`, and now `metadata={type}` if provided.
   - Call `enqueueEpisodeProcessing(episode.id)` — pushes a job onto the BullMQ `memory-ingestion` queue. Job options: 3 attempts, exponential backoff.
4. Respond `202 { episodeId, status: "queued" }`.

The whole request never makes an LLM call. The user sees ~5 ms latency.

### What I changed here

> **Tier 1.3 — episode type discriminator.** Added optional `type: "message" | "text" | "json"` to the request schema (`ingest.validator.ts`) and stored it in the existing `metadata` jsonb column (`episodes.service.ts`). Also added an `episodeType(ep)` accessor that defaults to `"message"` when the field is absent.
>
> **Why it matters:** The paper (§2.1) names three episode types. The system was hard-coded to `message`. Now the API accepts the other two without breaking existing clients, and the type is durably persisted for the worker (and future-you) to branch on. No DB migration required because metadata is jsonb.

---

## Phase 2 — Worker boot (one-time, at process start)

**File:** `workers/ingestion.worker.ts`

A separate Bun process spawns a BullMQ `Worker` listening on `memory-ingestion`, with `concurrency: 4` and a Redis connection from `queues/connection.ts`. Each job runs `processEpisode(job)` and is wrapped by:

- `step(episodeId, stepName, fn)` — records `processing_logs` row per step with duration and status.
- `worker.on("failed", ...)` — on final attempt, sets `episodes.status="failed"`.

This decouples API latency from LLM latency: the user already has their `episodeId` while the pipeline below runs in the background.

### What I changed here

Nothing. The harness was already solid.

---

## Phase 3 — Fetch episode + recent context (Postgres reads, ~20 ms)

**File:** `workers/ingestion.worker.ts` → `services/episodes.service.ts`

```ts
await db.update(episodes).set({ status: "processing" }).where(...)
const episode = await fetchEpisodeById(episodeId)
const recent  = await getRecentMessages(episode.sessionId, 6, episodeId)
```

`getRecentMessages` returns the last 6 messages from the same session, excluding the current one, in chronological order. This is the "rolling context window" that all downstream LLM prompts get.

> The paper uses `n = 4` ("two complete conversation turns"). The code uses `n = 6`. Not changed — both work and the larger window helps with pronoun resolution.

### What I changed here

Nothing. This phase already produced the right shape.

---

## Phase 4 — Entity extraction (LLM, ~600 ms)

**File:** `services/extraction.service.ts` → `extractEntities()`

`extractEntities(current, recent)` runs one OpenAI call with `gpt-4o-mini` and a structured-output JSON prompt. The system prompt instructs:
- Extract every distinct named thing AND every concept the speaker has a relation to (likes, owns, builds).
- Use canonical full names; resolve pronouns from `recent` context.
- **Do not extract the speaker themselves** — they are tracked separately.
- Return strictly `{ entities: [{ name, type, summary }] }` validated by `EntityListSchema`.

Output: `RawEntity[]` — each entry is `{ name, type ∈ {PERSON,COMPANY,LOCATION,CONCEPT,PRODUCT,DATE,OTHER}, summary }`.

### What I changed here

> **Tier 2.6 — reflexion pass (flag `ZEP_LLM_REFLEXION=1`).** Added `reflectOnEntities(current, recent, draft)` — a second LLM call that takes the draft list and asks a critic to return `{ add: [...], remove: [...] }`. The result is merged: `remove`-named entries are filtered out by case-insensitive name match; `add` entries are appended only if not already present.
>
> The flag check happens at the bottom of `extractEntities`:
> ```ts
> if (process.env.ZEP_LLM_REFLEXION === "1") {
>   return reflectOnEntities(current, recent, draft);
> }
> return draft;
> ```
>
> **Why it matters:** Paper §2.2.1 calls this out explicitly — "we employ a reflection technique inspired by reflexion to minimize hallucinations and enhance extraction coverage." A single LLM call sometimes misses concepts the speaker hints at obliquely ("I'm into climbing these days"), and sometimes extracts noise ("yesterday", "the thing"). A critic pass with a clean slate catches both classes of error.
>
> **How it fails safe:** On JSON parse error, schema mismatch, or any exception, the function logs and returns the unmodified `draft`. So enabling the flag never produces a worse-than-baseline outcome — only the same or better.

---

## Phase 5 — Entity resolution (Neo4j read + possibly Pinecone + LLM, ~30 ms → ~1.5 s)

**File:** `services/resolver.service.ts` → `resolveEntities()` and `getOrCreateSelfEntity()`

The pipeline now has two paths:

**Path A — name-normalized exact match (always runs, the cheap fast path):**

1. For each raw entity, compute `normalizedName = name.trim().toLowerCase().replace(/\s+/g, ' ')`.
2. De-dup the batch by normalized name (keep first occurrence).
3. Query Neo4j: `MATCH (e:Entity {userId}) WHERE e.normalizedName IN $names RETURN e`.
4. If a hit exists → resolved, `isNew: false`.
5. If no hit → continue to Path B (if flag on) or mint a new UUID.

**Path B — hybrid semantic resolution (flag-gated, new):**

Runs only for entities that did NOT match on Path A.

1. `embed(name + ": " + summary)` via OpenAI `text-embedding-3-small`.
2. `queryVectorsByEmbedding` against Pinecone with `filter: { userId, type: "entity" }`, topK=5.
3. Keep matches with score ≥ 0.55.
4. `findEntitiesByIds` to fetch full Neo4j props (name, type, summary) for those candidate IDs.
5. **Type-filter:** drop candidates whose `type` differs from the raw entity's type. A PERSON named "Apple" is never merged with the COMPANY "Apple".
6. Send the survivors + the new entity to `llmJudgeDuplicate`, which uses the paper's Appendix 6.1.2 prompt and returns `{ is_duplicate, existingEntityId, canonicalName }`.
7. If `is_duplicate: true` with a valid `existingEntityId`, reuse that entity ID, optionally upgrading its `name` to the canonical form. Otherwise fall through to mint a new UUID.

Finally, new entities are written via `upsertEntities`. Existing entities resolved on either path become `ResolvedEntity { ...entity, isNew: false }`.

Separately, `getOrCreateSelfEntity(userId)` always runs, idempotently upserting a `User` entity whose `entityId = userId`. This is the speaker.

### What I changed here

> **Tier 2.7 — hybrid entity resolution (flag `ZEP_LLM_ENTITY_RESOLVE=1`).** Added `semanticResolve(userId, raw)` plus `llmJudgeDuplicate(raw, candidates)` in `resolver.service.ts`, plus supporting helpers `queryVectorsByEmbedding` (`pinecone.service.ts`) and `findEntitiesByIds` (`neo4j.service.ts`).
>
> **Why it matters:** Before this change, "Apple" and "Apple Inc." would always become two separate entity nodes — `normalizedName` differs by " Inc.". So would "Kevin" and "Kevin Smith", or "Anthropic" and "the AI safety company". This is the paper's §2.2.1 hybrid approach: cosine + LLM verification, exactly what's described.
>
> **How it fails safe:** The Path A exact-name match is unchanged. Path B only adds *additional* dedup opportunities for entities that would otherwise become new nodes. If the LLM errors or returns `is_duplicate: false`, the code falls through to the original "create new" branch. The type-filter prevents the most dangerous failure mode — cross-type merges.

---

## Phase 6 — Fact extraction (LLM, ~700 ms)

**File:** `services/extraction.service.ts` → `extractFacts()`

`extractFacts(current, recent, entities)` runs one OpenAI call where the prompt:
- Lists all resolved entities (incl. the speaker as `u1 → "User"`).
- Asks for two arrays:
  - `facts: [{ sourceEntityId, targetEntityId, relationType, factText, confidence }]` — new relationships.
  - `invalidations: [{ sourceEntityId, targetEntityId, relationType }]` — relationships the message says have ENDED.
- Forces `relationType` into `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` via a zod regex `/^[A-Z][A-Z0-9_]*$/`.
- Forbids self-loops and forbids entity IDs not in the supplied list.
- Critically, handles negation: "I left X" becomes an entry in `invalidations` for `WORKS_AT`, **not** a positive fact `LEFT_COMPANY`. This is what makes contradiction detection clean downstream.

After the LLM response, the worker filters to valid entity IDs and drops self-loops as a defensive pass.

### What I changed here

Nothing for the extraction itself. But the *downstream consumer* of `facts` and `invalidations` (the next phase) is heavily upgraded.

---

## Phase 7 — Temporal application (the biggest phase; ~50 ms → ~5 s depending on flags)

**File:** `services/temporal.service.ts` → `applyTemporalFacts()`

This is where the temporal knowledge-graph semantics live. The worker now calls it with an extra arg:

```ts
applyTemporalFacts(facts, invalidations, episodeId, occurredAtIso,
                   { current: currentMsg, recent });
```

The fifth arg is the new `TemporalContext` — only used when `ZEP_LLM_TEMPORAL=1`.

### Step 7a — LLM temporal extraction *(flag-gated, new)*

> **Tier 2.5 (flag `ZEP_LLM_TEMPORAL=1`):** Before any writes, for each new fact in parallel:
> ```ts
> const t = await extractTemporal(fact.factText, ctx.current, ctx.recent, occurredAt)
> // → { validAt: string | null, invalidAt: string | null }
> ```
> Implements paper Appendix 6.1.5 verbatim — the prompt knows that the reference timestamp anchors relative dates ("two weeks ago", "last summer") and parses absolute dates ("June 23, 1912"). Results are stored in `temporalByIndex` and consumed below when constructing each `GraphFact`.
>
> **Why it matters:** Without this, every fact gets `validFrom = episode.occurredAt`. A message that says "I started at Anthropic two years ago" produces a fact with `validFrom = now`, which is wrong — it should be `validFrom = now - 2y`. With the flag on, the LLM resolves the relative date against `occurredAt`.

### Step 7b — Rule-based contradiction closure (always on; existing)

For each new fact, finds open edges (`validUntil IS NULL`) on the same `(sourceEntityId, relationType)`. If the existing edge's target ≠ the new target, the existing edge is closed: `closeFact(factId, occurredAt)`.

Example: existing `User -[WORKS_AT]-> Grok` is open. New message says `User -[WORKS_AT]-> Anthropic`. The old edge gets `validUntil = occurredAt` and is preserved (history retained); the new edge is written.

### Step 7c — Write new facts, with the reaffirmation + dedup branching

The write loop iterates over `rawFacts`. For each fact, it picks one of four paths:

**Path 7c-i — Reaffirmation (existing path, but rewired):**

If an open edge already exists with the same `(source, relationType, target)` triple, this is the same fact stated again.

> **Tier 1.2 change.** This used to be:
> ```ts
> if (reaffirmation) { skipped++; continue; }
> ```
> The information just got dropped. Now it's:
> ```ts
> if (reaffirmation) {
>   writes.push(reinforceFact(reaffirmation.factId, episodeId, occurredAt));
>   skipped++;
>   continue;
> }
> ```
> `reinforceFact` (new helper in `neo4j.service.ts`) does three idempotent things in one Cypher statement:
> 1. Appends `episodeId` to the edge's `episodeIds` list (skipping if already present).
> 2. Bumps `confidence` by 0.01 (capped at 1.0).
> 3. Updates `lastSeenAt = datetime(occurredAt)`.
>
> **Why it matters:** Multi-episode provenance — paper §2.1 talks about "bidirectional indices that track the relationships between edges and their source episodes." Previously each `:FACT` only knew its originating episode; now it accumulates every episode that asserted it. This becomes the basis for citation, "show me everywhere the user said this," and the recency / strength signals retrieval can use.

**Path 7c-ii — Pair-scoped semantic dedup (flag-gated, new):**

> **Tier 2.9 (flag `ZEP_LLM_FACT_DEDUP=1`):** Before writing, query `findFactsBySourceAndTarget(src, tgt)` to find open edges between exactly this pair. If any exist, embed the new `factText` and each candidate's `factText`, compute cosine similarities. If the best score ≥ 0.85, treat as a reaffirmation of THAT edge — call `reinforceFact` and skip the write.
>
> **Why it matters:** Two LLM calls on two different messages might produce `relationType: WORKS_AT` and `relationType: EMPLOYED_BY` for the same underlying fact. Path 7c-i misses it because `relationType` differs. With this flag, the system recognizes the same underlying relationship by semantic similarity of the prose form and merges into a single edge.

**Path 7c-iii — Write a new edge (default path):**

If neither reaffirmation path fires, mint a new `factId = randomUUID()` and call `upsertFact(fact)`.

> **Tier 1.1 change.** `upsertFact`'s Cypher now also sets:
> - `f.tCreated = datetime()` — paper's `t'_created`, transaction-time creation marker, distinct from `f.validFrom` which is event-time.
> - `f.episodeIds = [$episodeId]` — initializes the provenance list.
> - `f.lastSeenAt = datetime($validFrom)` — for recency scoring.
>
> **Why it matters:** The paper specifies bi-temporal — `(t_valid, t_invalid)` is event time (when the fact held true in reality) and `(t'_created, t'_expired)` is transaction time (when the system learned about the fact). Before this change only event time existed, which makes it impossible to answer "what did the system believe about X on March 1st, before the user corrected itself in April?". Transaction time is the audit dimension that makes the system replay-able.

Also note: `validFrom` and `validUntil` now use the Tier 2.5 LLM results when the flag is on:
```ts
const t = temporalByIndex.get(i);
const validFrom = t?.validAt ?? occurredAt;
const validUntil = t?.invalidAt ?? null;
```

The `??` fallbacks mean: if the LLM returned `null` for a field (or the flag is off, so the map is empty), the existing default kicks in. No regression.

### Step 7d — Explicit invalidations (always on; existing)

For each entry in `invalidations[]` (returned by the fact-extraction LLM as "this previously-true relation has ended"), find every matching open edge and call `closeFact`.

> **Tier 1.1 also applies to closures.** `closeFact` Cypher now does:
> ```cypher
> SET f.validUntil = datetime($validUntil),
>     f.tExpired   = coalesce(f.tExpired, datetime())
> ```
> So every close stamps the transaction-time `tExpired` marker the first time. The `coalesce` makes re-closes a no-op (idempotent).

### Step 7e — Semantic invalidation pass (flag-gated, new)

> **Tier 2.8 (flag `ZEP_LLM_INVALIDATE=1`):**
>
> ```ts
> if (process.env.ZEP_LLM_INVALIDATE === "1" && rawFacts.length > 0) {
>   const alreadyClosed = new Set([...closed, ...invalidationCloses].map(c => c.factId));
>   const newlyClosedIds = await semanticInvalidate(rawFacts, alreadyClosed, occurredAt);
>   ...
> }
> ```
>
> `semanticInvalidate` works per source entity. For each source involved in the new facts:
> 1. Fetch ALL open edges from that source: `findOpenFactsForSource(sourceId)`.
> 2. Exclude edges already closed by Steps 7b / 7d (the `alreadyClosed` set).
> 3. Exclude edges that match the new fact's `(relationType, target)` exactly (those are handled by the rule path).
> 4. Embed the new fact's text AND each remaining candidate's `factText`. Compute cosine similarities.
> 5. Take top-5 candidates with similarity ≥ 0.45.
> 6. For each, call `llmJudgeContradiction(newText, oldText)` — a focused prompt that returns `{ is_contradiction: boolean }`. The prompt is given a few examples of contradictions vs. compatible facts (`"likes dogs"` vs `"likes cats"` is NOT a contradiction; `"lives in Boston"` vs `"lives in Tokyo"` IS).
> 7. On `true`, call `closeFact` and track the ID in `semanticClosed`.
>
> **Why it matters:** Paper §2.2.3: "The system employs an LLM to compare new edges against semantically related existing edges to identify potential contradictions." Before this change, only exact `(source, relationType)` overlaps triggered closure. Real contradictions cross relationType boundaries — `LIVES_IN` vs `RESIDES_IN`, `WORKS_AT` vs `EMPLOYED_BY`, `MARRIED_TO` vs `DIVORCED_FROM`. The rule path misses all of these. The semantic pass catches them via embedding + LLM-as-judge.
>
> **How it fails safe:** The pass only ever CLOSES edges, never writes new ones. It operates on the remainder after rule-based closures, so it can only close *more* than the baseline. If embedding fails, the LLM judge errors, or the LLM returns `false`, nothing closes. The baseline-behavior is always achievable.

The return shape is `{ written, closed: [...rule, ...explicit, ...semantic], skipped }`. The worker logs counts of each.

---

## Phase 8 — Vector indexing (Pinecone, ~400 ms; two batched calls)

**File:** `services/pinecone.service.ts`

Two upserts run as `Promise.all`:

### `upsertEntityVectors(resolved)`

Only `e.isNew === true` entities get embedded. The vector text is `${name}: ${summary}`. Metadata:
```json
{ "type": "entity", "userId": "...", "neo4jId": "...",
  "name": "...", "entityType": "PERSON" }
```

### `upsertFactVectors(userId, temporal.written)`

Each new fact gets embedded with `factText` as the vector text.

> **Tier 1.4 change.** Metadata now includes:
> ```ts
> {
>   type: "fact",
>   userId, neo4jId,
>   sourceId, targetId, relationType, factText,
>   validFromMs,                              // ← NEW
>   isOpen: validUntilMs === null,            // ← NEW
>   ...(validUntilMs !== null ? { validUntilMs } : {})  // ← NEW (omitted when open)
> }
> ```
> Pinecone metadata only takes scalar types, so dates are stored as epoch milliseconds and a boolean `isOpen` carries the null-vs-set state.
>
> **Why it matters:** Retrieval can now filter at the vector layer (`filter: { userId, isOpen: true }` or `validFromMs: { $lte: asOfMs }`) without needing the graph hop for coarse temporal pruning. PLAN §4 / §6 specifically called for this and it was missing.

---

## Phase 9 — Graph linking (Neo4j writes, ~30 ms)

**File:** `services/neo4j.service.ts`

Sequential after the vectors:

```ts
await createEpisodeNode({ episodeId, userId, sessionId, actor, content, occurredAt })
await linkEpisodeToEntities(episodeId, [self, ...resolved].map(e => e.entityId), occurredAt)
```

- `createEpisodeNode` MERGEs an `:Episode` node by `episodeId`.
- `linkEpisodeToEntities` MERGEs `(ep)-[:MENTIONS {timestamp}]->(ent)` for each entity (self + every extracted entity, including resolved-not-new ones).

This is what makes traversal queries like "show me every entity ever mentioned in session X" or "trace this fact back to the message that introduced it" tractable.

### What I changed here

Nothing in this phase — the existing graph wiring was correct.

---

## Phase 10 — Completion

```ts
await db.update(episodes)
  .set({ status: "processed", processedAt: new Date() })
  .where(eq(episodes.id, episodeId))
```

Plus a `[worker] completed {id}` log. The `processing_logs` table now has one row per step (ten or so per episode), each with `duration_ms` — that's the audit trail / debugging surface.

---

# Summary: what changed and what it bought

| Phase | Change | Status before | Status after | Net effect |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Episode type discriminator | Hard-coded `message` | `message`/`text`/`json` supported, stored in `metadata` | API matches paper §2.1; no DB migration |
| 4 | Reflexion pass on entity extraction | One-shot LLM | Optional critic pass via `ZEP_LLM_REFLEXION=1` | Recovers missed entities, drops hallucinated ones |
| 5 | Hybrid entity resolution | Normalized-name match only | Optional cosine + LLM via `ZEP_LLM_ENTITY_RESOLVE=1` | "Apple" merges with "Apple Inc."; type-filtered to prevent bad merges |
| 7a | LLM temporal extraction per fact | `validFrom = occurredAt`, always | Optional LLM-derived `validAt` / `invalidAt` via `ZEP_LLM_TEMPORAL=1` | Relative dates ("two years ago") and absolute dates ("in 2018") resolve correctly |
| 7b | Bi-temporal: transaction time | Only event-time `(validFrom, validUntil)` | Plus `tCreated` on insert and `tExpired` on close | Audit / replay-ability — "what did the system know on date X" |
| 7c-i | Multi-episode provenance | `episodeId: string` (one) | `episodeIds: list` accumulated via `reinforceFact` + `lastSeenAt` + bumped `confidence` | Reaffirmations strengthen facts instead of being dropped; can cite every supporting message |
| 7c-ii | Pair-scoped semantic dedup | Same (source, target) with different `relationType` → two edges | Optional cosine ≥ 0.85 merge via `ZEP_LLM_FACT_DEDUP=1` | Stops the graph from getting littered with `WORKS_AT`+`EMPLOYED_BY` clones |
| 7e | Semantic edge invalidation | Only exact `(source, relationType)` overlaps closed | Optional cosine + LLM contradiction judge via `ZEP_LLM_INVALIDATE=1` | Catches `LIVES_IN` vs `RESIDES_IN`-style contradictions the rule path misses |
| 8 | Pinecone fact metadata | No temporal fields on vectors | `validFromMs`, `validUntilMs`, `isOpen` mirrored | Retrieval can prune by time at the vector layer instead of always hopping through Neo4j |

Five flags, each independently toggleable in `.env.local`:

```
ZEP_LLM_TEMPORAL=1
ZEP_LLM_REFLEXION=1
ZEP_LLM_ENTITY_RESOLVE=1
ZEP_LLM_INVALIDATE=1
ZEP_LLM_FACT_DEDUP=1
```

With every flag off, the pipeline is identical in behavior to the pre-change version (plus the always-on Tier 1 additions: episodeIds, tCreated/tExpired, Pinecone temporal metadata, episode type). With every flag on, the pipeline implements every paper §2 idea except the community subgraph (which I deferred to a Tier 3 task — that one needs a separate worker and queue, and would have meaningfully changed behavior).

Each flag's failure mode is `log + return baseline` — there is no flag that, when its LLM call errors, makes the pipeline produce a worse result than the flag being off.

## C. Exercising the always-on Tier 1 changes

### Multi-episode provenance — reaffirmation appends + bumps confidence

```bash
# same content twice
curl ... -d "{\"sessionId\":\"$S\",\"actor\":\"user\",\"content\":\"I work at Anthropic.\"}"
sleep 4
curl ... -d "{\"sessionId\":\"$S\",\"actor\":\"user\",\"content\":\"Yeah, I'm at Anthropic.\"}"
```

In Neo4j after both episodes process:
```cypher
MATCH (u:Entity {type:"PERSON",name:"User"})-[f:FACT {relationType:"WORKS_AT"}]->(c:Entity {name:"Anthropic"})
RETURN f.episodeIds, size(f.episodeIds) AS n, f.confidence, f.lastSeenAt
```
Expect `n = 2`, `confidence` bumped by 0.01 (capped at 1.0), `lastSeenAt` updated to the second episode's time. Before my changes there would be either (a) a single-episode fact with the second silently dropped, or (b) two duplicate edges.

### Bi-temporal closure — `tExpired` is set on contradiction

```bash
curl ... -d "{\"sessionId\":\"$S\",\"actor\":\"user\",\"content\":\"I work at Grok.\"}"
sleep 4
curl ... -d "{\"sessionId\":\"$S\",\"actor\":\"user\",\"content\":\"I left Grok and joined Anthropic.\"}"
```

```cypher
MATCH (u:Entity {name:"User"})-[f:FACT {relationType:"WORKS_AT"}]->(c)
RETURN c.name, f.validFrom, f.validUntil, f.tCreated, f.tExpired
```
Expect two edges to `Grok` and `Anthropic`. The Grok edge has `validUntil` and `tExpired` both set (event-time end vs. system-learned time — they'll be close but `tExpired` is the moment the worker processed, not the moment the user said it).

## D. Exercising each Tier 2 flag

Flags live in `server/.env.local`. After editing, **restart the worker** (the env var is read per-call but the worker process re-reads on start — re-source if you've got it open).

| Flag | What to send to trigger | How to verify in Neo4j |
|---|---|---|
| `ZEP_LLM_REFLEXION=1` | A message with a subtle concept the speaker has a relation to, e.g. `"I've been getting really into bouldering lately"` | Without flag: maybe no entity for "bouldering". With flag: `Bouldering [CONCEPT]` shows up + `User -[LIKES/ENJOYS]-> Bouldering`. |
| `ZEP_LLM_ENTITY_RESOLVE=1` | First message: `"I work at Apple."` Wait. Second message in a new session: `"I'm an engineer at Apple Inc."` | Without flag: two entities (`Apple`, `Apple Inc.`). With flag: one entity, two `:MENTIONS` edges, single `WORKS_AT` fact. |
| `ZEP_LLM_TEMPORAL=1` | `"I started at Anthropic two years ago."` | Without flag: `f.validFrom = episode.occurredAt` (today). With flag: `f.validFrom ≈ today - 2 years`. |
| `ZEP_LLM_INVALIDATE=1` | First: `"I live in Boston."` Then later: `"My place in Tokyo is great."` (note: different relationType wording would defeat the rule path — use synonyms to be more telling) | Without flag: both edges open. With flag: the Boston edge gets `validUntil` set on the Tokyo episode's `occurredAt`. |
| `ZEP_LLM_FACT_DEDUP=1` | Tricky to trigger naturally because the fact-extraction LLM tends to produce the same `relationType` for a given pair. Force it by using two phrasings that the prompt converts differently: `"I work at Anthropic"` vs `"I'm employed by Anthropic"`. | Without flag: two edges (`WORKS_AT`, `EMPLOYED_BY`). With flag: one edge reinforced; check `episodeIds` is length 2. |

To compare baseline vs. flag-on, the cleanest pattern:

```bash
# baseline
bun run worker   # no flags set
# ingest, verify
# stop worker

# flagged
ZEP_LLM_REFLEXION=1 bun run worker
# ingest the same content again under a NEW user (signup again) to avoid cross-contamination
# verify
```

Cleanup between runs:
```cypher
// in Neo4j Browser
MATCH (n) WHERE n.userId = $userId DETACH DELETE n
```
```bash
# Pinecone — by metadata filter via the API, or in the console
```

## E. Watch the worker log

The worker prints a one-line summary per episode showing the new branches firing:

```
[abc123de] start "I work at Anthropic..."
[abc123de] recent: 0 prior message(s) loaded
[abc123de] extracted 1 entity: Anthropic[COMPANY]
[abc123de] resolved 1 (1 new): Anthropic*
[abc123de] facts (1): User-WORKS_AT->Anthropic
[abc123de] invalidations (0): (none)
[abc123de] temporal: written=1 closed=0 skipped=0     ← skipped will jump on reaffirmation
[abc123de] pinecone: 1 entity vector(s), 1 fact vector(s) upserted
[abc123de] graph: episode node + 2 :MENTIONS edge(s) written
[abc123de] done in 1840ms
```

Anything that errors inside an LLM-flagged path will show up in `processing_logs` with `status="error"` for the corresponding step. Quick query:

```sql
SELECT step, status, message, duration_ms
FROM processing_logs
WHERE episode_id = '...'
ORDER BY created_at;
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```typescript
// Tier 2 flag-gated path smoke test.
// Toggles each ZEP_LLM_* flag at runtime, drives a targeted fixture through
// the relevant service, and asserts the flagged-vs-baseline difference.
// Costs ~$0.01 in OpenAI API per run.
import { randomUUID } from "node:crypto";
import { neo4jDriver } from "../src/utils/neo4j";
import {
  findFactsBySourceAndTarget,
  findOpenFactsForSource,
  upsertEntities,
  upsertEntity,
  upsertFact,
  type GraphEntity,
  type GraphFact,
} from "../src/services/neo4j.service";
import { applyTemporalFacts } from "../src/services/temporal.service";
import { resolveEntities } from "../src/services/resolver.service";
import {
  extractEntities,
  type RawEntity,
  type RawFact,
} from "../src/services/extraction.service";
import { upsertEntityVectors } from "../src/services/pinecone.service";

const TEST_USER_ID = "00000000-0000-0000-0000-0000000000a1";

let passed = 0;
let failed = 0;

function assert(cond: unknown, msg: string) {
  if (!cond) {
    console.error(`  ✗ ${msg}`);
    failed++;
    process.exitCode = 1;
  } else {
    console.log(`  ✓ ${msg}`);
    passed++;
  }
}

function setFlag(name: string, on: boolean) {
  if (on) process.env[name] = "1";
  else delete process.env[name];
}

async function cleanup() {
  const session = neo4jDriver.session();
  try {
    await session.run(
      `MATCH (n) WHERE n.userId = $userId DETACH DELETE n`,
      { userId: TEST_USER_ID },
    );
  } finally {
    await session.close();
  }
}

async function deleteEntityById(entityId: string) {
  const session = neo4jDriver.session();
  try {
    await session.run(
      `MATCH (e:Entity { entityId: $entityId }) DETACH DELETE e`,
      { entityId },
    );
  } finally {
    await session.close();
  }
}

function sleep(ms: number) {
  return new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, ms));
}

async function ensureUser(): Promise<GraphEntity> {
  const self: GraphEntity = {
    entityId: TEST_USER_ID,
    userId: TEST_USER_ID,
    name: "User",
    normalizedName: "user",
    type: "PERSON",
    summary: "The user (speaker).",
  };
  await upsertEntity(self);
  return self;
}

// ─── Tier 2.5: LLM temporal extraction ───────────────────────────────
async function testTemporalExtraction() {
  console.log("\n==== Tier 2.5: ZEP_LLM_TEMPORAL ====");
  await cleanup();
  await ensureUser();
  const anthropic: GraphEntity = {
    entityId: randomUUID(),
    userId: TEST_USER_ID,
    name: "Anthropic",
    normalizedName: "anthropic",
    type: "COMPANY",
    summary: "An AI safety company.",
  };
  await upsertEntity(anthropic);

  const occurredAt = "2026-05-12T10:00:00.000Z";
  const facts: RawFact[] = [
    {
      sourceEntityId: TEST_USER_ID,
      targetEntityId: anthropic.entityId,
      relationType: "WORKS_AT",
      factText: "The user started working at Anthropic two years ago.",
      confidence: 0.99,
    },
  ];
  const ctx = {
    current: {
      actor: "user" as const,
      content: "I started at Anthropic two years ago.",
    },
    recent: [],
  };

  // Baseline: flag off → validFrom = occurredAt
  setFlag("ZEP_LLM_TEMPORAL", false);
  await applyTemporalFacts(facts, [], randomUUID(), occurredAt, ctx);
  let opens = await findOpenFactsForSource(TEST_USER_ID);
  assert(opens.length === 1, "baseline: 1 open fact");
  console.log(`    baseline validFrom: ${opens[0].validFrom}`);
  assert(
    opens[0].validFrom.startsWith("2026-05-12"),
    "baseline: validFrom = occurredAt (no LLM rewrite)",
  );

  // Flag on → validFrom shifted backwards
  await cleanup();
  await ensureUser();
  await upsertEntity(anthropic);

  setFlag("ZEP_LLM_TEMPORAL", true);
  await applyTemporalFacts(facts, [], randomUUID(), occurredAt, ctx);
  setFlag("ZEP_LLM_TEMPORAL", false);

  opens = await findOpenFactsForSource(TEST_USER_ID);
  assert(opens.length === 1, "flagged: 1 open fact");
  console.log(`    flagged  validFrom: ${opens[0].validFrom}`);
  const year = parseInt(opens[0].validFrom.slice(0, 4), 10);
  assert(
    year <= 2024,
    `flagged: validFrom year ≤ 2024 (got ${year}; "two years ago" from 2026-05)`,
  );
}

// ─── Tier 2.6: reflexion on entity extraction ────────────────────────
async function testReflexion() {
  console.log("\n==== Tier 2.6: ZEP_LLM_REFLEXION ====");
  const msg = {
    actor: "user" as const,
    content:
      "I've been getting really into bouldering lately, and also reading more philosophy.",
  };

  setFlag("ZEP_LLM_REFLEXION", false);
  const baseline = await extractEntities(msg);
  console.log(
    `    baseline (${baseline.length}): ${baseline.map((e) => `${e.name}[${e.type}]`).join(", ") || "(none)"}`,
  );

  setFlag("ZEP_LLM_REFLEXION", true);
  const flagged = await extractEntities(msg);
  setFlag("ZEP_LLM_REFLEXION", false);
  console.log(
    `    flagged  (${flagged.length}): ${flagged.map((e) => `${e.name}[${e.type}]`).join(", ") || "(none)"}`,
  );

  // Reflexion is non-deterministic. Only assert the path runs and returns
  // well-formed entities — never assert > or < count, since LLMs vary.
  assert(Array.isArray(flagged), "flagged: returns array");
  assert(
    flagged.every(
      (e) =>
        typeof e.name === "string" &&
        e.name.length > 0 &&
        typeof e.type === "string" &&
        typeof e.summary === "string",
    ),
    "flagged: each entity well-formed (name+type+summary)",
  );
}

// ─── Tier 2.7: hybrid entity resolution (cosine + LLM) ───────────────
async function testHybridResolve() {
  console.log("\n==== Tier 2.7: ZEP_LLM_ENTITY_RESOLVE ====");
  await cleanup();

  // Seed: create "Anthropic" via the real resolver path + put vector in Pinecone
  const rawSeed: RawEntity[] = [
    {
      name: "Anthropic",
      type: "COMPANY",
      summary: "An AI safety company that builds Claude.",
    },
  ];
  const seeded = await resolveEntities(TEST_USER_ID, rawSeed);
  assert(seeded.length === 1 && seeded[0].isNew, "seed: Anthropic created NEW");
  const anthropicId = seeded[0].entityId;
  await upsertEntityVectors(seeded);
  console.log(`    waiting 2s for Pinecone propagation...`);
  await sleep(2000);

  const rawNew: RawEntity[] = [
    {
      name: "Anthropic Inc.",
      type: "COMPANY",
      summary: "The AI safety lab building Claude.",
    },
  ];

  // Baseline: flag off → normalized name differs → new entity
  setFlag("ZEP_LLM_ENTITY_RESOLVE", false);
  const baseline = await resolveEntities(TEST_USER_ID, rawNew);
  assert(baseline.length === 1, "baseline: 1 resolved");
  assert(baseline[0].isNew, "baseline: created NEW (no hybrid path)");
  assert(
    baseline[0].entityId !== anthropicId,
    "baseline: different entityId from seed",
  );
  // Remove the duplicate so the flagged path has a clean starting state
  await deleteEntityById(baseline[0].entityId);

  // Flag on → should resolve back to the seed
  setFlag("ZEP_LLM_ENTITY_RESOLVE", true);
  const flagged = await resolveEntities(TEST_USER_ID, rawNew);
  setFlag("ZEP_LLM_ENTITY_RESOLVE", false);
  assert(flagged.length === 1, "flagged: 1 resolved");
  console.log(
    `    flagged: entityId=${flagged[0].entityId.slice(0, 8)}.. isNew=${flagged[0].isNew} name="${flagged[0].name}"`,
  );
  assert(!flagged[0].isNew, "flagged: resolved to EXISTING entity");
  assert(
    flagged[0].entityId === anthropicId,
    'flagged: same entityId as seed ("Anthropic" ≡ "Anthropic Inc.")',
  );
}

// ─── Tier 2.8: semantic edge invalidation ────────────────────────────
async function testSemanticInvalidation() {
  console.log("\n==== Tier 2.8: ZEP_LLM_INVALIDATE ====");
  await cleanup();
  await ensureUser();

  const boston: GraphEntity = {
    entityId: randomUUID(),
    userId: TEST_USER_ID,
    name: "Boston",
    normalizedName: "boston",
    type: "LOCATION",
    summary: "A city in Massachusetts.",
  };
  const tokyo: GraphEntity = {
    entityId: randomUUID(),
    userId: TEST_USER_ID,
    name: "Tokyo",
    normalizedName: "tokyo",
    type: "LOCATION",
    summary: "The capital of Japan.",
  };
  await upsertEntities([boston, tokyo]);

  function makeSeedFact(): GraphFact {
    return {
      factId: randomUUID(),
      sourceEntityId: TEST_USER_ID,
      targetEntityId: boston.entityId,
      relationType: "LIVES_IN",
      factText: "The user lives in Boston.",
      validFrom: "2026-01-01T00:00:00.000Z",
      validUntil: null,
      confidence: 0.99,
      episodeId: randomUUID(),
    };
  }
  await upsertFact(makeSeedFact());

  // New fact: same idea, different relationType ⇒ rule path can't catch it
  const newFact: RawFact = {
    sourceEntityId: TEST_USER_ID,
    targetEntityId: tokyo.entityId,
    relationType: "RESIDES_IN",
    factText: "The user resides in Tokyo.",
    confidence: 0.99,
  };
  const occurredAt = "2026-05-12T10:00:00.000Z";
  const ctx = {
    current: { actor: "user" as const, content: "I now live in Tokyo." },
    recent: [],
  };

  // Baseline: rule misses cross-predicate contradiction
  setFlag("ZEP_LLM_INVALIDATE", false);
  await applyTemporalFacts([newFact], [], randomUUID(), occurredAt, ctx);
  let opens = await findOpenFactsForSource(TEST_USER_ID);
  assert(
    opens.find((o) => o.relationType === "LIVES_IN") !== undefined,
    "baseline: Boston/LIVES_IN STILL open (rule path can't see cross-predicate)",
  );
  assert(
    opens.find((o) => o.relationType === "RESIDES_IN") !== undefined,
    "baseline: Tokyo/RESIDES_IN written",
  );

  // Reset + flag on
  await cleanup();
  await ensureUser();
  await upsertEntities([boston, tokyo]);
  await upsertFact(makeSeedFact());

  setFlag("ZEP_LLM_INVALIDATE", true);
  await applyTemporalFacts([newFact], [], randomUUID(), occurredAt, ctx);
  setFlag("ZEP_LLM_INVALIDATE", false);

  opens = await findOpenFactsForSource(TEST_USER_ID);
  console.log(`    open facts after flagged: ${opens.length}`);
  for (const o of opens) {
    console.log(`      ${o.relationType}: "${o.factText}"`);
  }
  assert(
    opens.find((o) => o.relationType === "LIVES_IN") === undefined,
    "flagged: Boston CLOSED via semantic invalidation (Tier 2.8)",
  );
  assert(
    opens.find((o) => o.relationType === "RESIDES_IN") !== undefined,
    "flagged: Tokyo still open",
  );
}

// ─── Tier 2.9: pair-scoped semantic fact dedup ───────────────────────
async function testPairScopedDedup() {
  console.log("\n==== Tier 2.9: ZEP_LLM_FACT_DEDUP ====");
  await cleanup();
  await ensureUser();

  const company: GraphEntity = {
    entityId: randomUUID(),
    userId: TEST_USER_ID,
    name: "Anthropic",
    normalizedName: "anthropic",
    type: "COMPANY",
    summary: "AI safety company.",
  };
  await upsertEntity(company);

  const seedFactId = randomUUID();
  function makeSeedFact(): GraphFact {
    return {
      factId: seedFactId,
      sourceEntityId: TEST_USER_ID,
      targetEntityId: company.entityId,
      relationType: "WORKS_AT",
      factText: "The user works at Anthropic.",
      validFrom: "2026-01-01T00:00:00.000Z",
      validUntil: null,
      confidence: 0.9,
      episodeId: randomUUID(),
    };
  }
  await upsertFact(makeSeedFact());

  // Same (source, target), different relationType, semantically equivalent text
  const newFact: RawFact = {
    sourceEntityId: TEST_USER_ID,
    targetEntityId: company.entityId,
    relationType: "EMPLOYED_BY",
    factText: "The user is employed by Anthropic.",
    confidence: 0.95,
  };
  const occurredAt = "2026-05-12T10:00:00.000Z";
  const ctx = {
    current: {
      actor: "user" as const,
      content: "I'm employed by Anthropic.",
    },
    recent: [],
  };

  // Baseline: two edges
  setFlag("ZEP_LLM_FACT_DEDUP", false);
  await applyTemporalFacts([newFact], [], randomUUID(), occurredAt, ctx);
  let pair = await findFactsBySourceAndTarget(TEST_USER_ID, company.entityId);
  assert(pair.length === 2, `baseline: 2 edges (got ${pair.length})`);

  // Reset + flag on
  await cleanup();
  await ensureUser();
  await upsertEntity(company);
  await upsertFact(makeSeedFact());

  setFlag("ZEP_LLM_FACT_DEDUP", true);
  const newEpId = randomUUID();
  await applyTemporalFacts([newFact], [], newEpId, occurredAt, ctx);
  setFlag("ZEP_LLM_FACT_DEDUP", false);

  pair = await findFactsBySourceAndTarget(TEST_USER_ID, company.entityId);
  console.log(`    edges after flagged: ${pair.length}`);
  for (const p of pair) console.log(`      ${p.relationType}: "${p.factText}"`);
  assert(pair.length === 1, "flagged: 1 edge (semantic dedup merged)");

  // Verify the merge reinforced the seed (not the other way around)
  const session = neo4jDriver.session();
  try {
    const result = await session.run(
      `MATCH ()-[f:FACT { factId: $factId }]->()
       RETURN coalesce(f.episodeIds, [f.episodeId]) AS eps,
              f.confidence AS conf,
              toString(f.lastSeenAt) AS lastSeenAt`,
      { factId: seedFactId },
    );
    const rec = result.records[0];
    if (!rec) {
      assert(false, "flagged: seed factId still present");
    } else {
      const eps = rec.get("eps") as string[];
      const conf = rec.get("conf") as number;
      const lastSeenAt = rec.get("lastSeenAt") as string;
      console.log(
        `    seed fact after reinforce: episodeIds=[${eps.length}], conf=${conf}, lastSeenAt=${lastSeenAt}`,
      );
      assert(
        eps.includes(newEpId),
        "flagged: seed.episodeIds contains the new episode",
      );
      assert(conf >= 0.91, "flagged: seed confidence bumped");
      assert(
        lastSeenAt?.startsWith("2026-05-12") ?? false,
        "flagged: seed lastSeenAt updated to new occurredAt",
      );
    }
  } finally {
    await session.close();
  }
}

async function main() {
  await testTemporalExtraction();
  await testReflexion();
  await testHybridResolve();
  await testSemanticInvalidation();
  await testPairScopedDedup();

  console.log("\nCleaning up...");
  await cleanup();

  console.log(`\n==== ${passed} passed, ${failed} failed ====`);
}

main()
  .catch((err) => {
    console.error(err);
    process.exitCode = 1;
  })
  .finally(() => neo4jDriver.close());

```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-12 15:10:38Z)**_

Yes — those are perfect. They hit every path I added. Here's the map first, then a runner script you can fire.

## What each message exercises

| Ingest | Always-on paths hit | Optional-flag paths hit |
|---|---|---|
| **1.** "I love dogs. Building memory system at Grok in SF" | Entity extract → resolver (4-5 new entities). New facts → `upsertFact` with `tCreated`, `episodeIds=[ep1]` (Tier 1.1, 1.2). Pinecone fact vectors get `validFromMs`, `isOpen` (Tier 1.4). | If `ZEP_LLM_REFLEXION=1`, the abstract "memory system" / "AI agent" concepts may surface. |
| **2.** "I love cats now, not dogs" | Rule-based contradiction: `User-LOVES-Dogs` closes (`validUntil` + `tExpired` set — Tier 1.1); new `User-LOVES-Cats` written. | — |
| **3.** "Yesterday I met Karthik at Anthropic. He used to work at OpenAI on Codex." | New entities (Karthik, Anthropic, OpenAI, Codex). "used to work at" → fact-extraction LLM should emit an explicit `invalidation` for `WORKS_AT` → `tExpired` set immediately on that edge. | **`ZEP_LLM_TEMPORAL=1`** triggers — "yesterday" resolves to `occurredAt - 1d`, so `validFrom` on the MET-Karthik fact is shifted backwards. |
| **4.** "My friend Mei lives in Tokyo, runs Aria" | Multi-fact extraction: `User-FRIENDS_WITH-Mei`, `Mei-LIVES_IN-Tokyo`, `Mei-FOUNDED/RUNS-Aria`. All get fresh `tCreated`. | — |
| **5.** "I'm not building the memory system anymore" | Explicit invalidation of `User-BUILDING-AI memory system` from message 1 → that edge gets `validUntil`+`tExpired`. | If `ZEP_LLM_INVALIDATE=1` was already on, this is likely caught even without the explicit "not anymore" phrasing. |

## What each query exercises

| Query | Expected answer | What's being tested |
|---|---|---|
| **"what does the user love"** | "The user loves cats" (Dogs is closed). | Temporal filter in `expandFromIds` (`coalesce(validUntil, '9999') >= asOf`) — Dogs should be excluded by default. Recency scoring puts Cats on top. |
| **"my friend in Japan"** | Mei + her startup Aria + Tokyo. | Graph hop: query matches `Tokyo` entity by name vector → 1-hop traversal picks up `Mei-LIVES_IN-Tokyo` and `Mei-FOUNDED-Aria`. Pure-vector RAG would miss this without the graph hop. |
| **"Karthik's work history"** | Used-to-work-at OpenAI on Codex. | Returns the CLOSED edge from message 3. `expandFromIds` returns history when `asOf` lets the closed range cover it; check what default `asOf` does. (If "currently true" only, this returns the LLM-extracted "used_to_work" relation type which is open or marked as past depending on the LLM.) |
| **"what is the user currently building"** | Nothing / "no longer building" — message 5 closed it. | Tests that message 5's invalidation actually took. Pre-message-5, this would return "AI memory system at Grok". After, the edge is closed and gets filtered by the `asOf` window. |

## Runner script

I'll drop a runnable scenario script and you can execute it whenever your server + worker are up.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-12 15:14:14Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```typescript
// End-to-end scenario test against the running HTTP API.
//
// Prereqs (in two other terminals):
//   bun run dev      (server on :3002)
//   bun run worker
//
// Optional flags — set in .env.local BEFORE starting worker:
//   ZEP_LLM_TEMPORAL=1 ZEP_LLM_INVALIDATE=1 ZEP_LLM_FACT_DEDUP=1
//   ZEP_LLM_REFLEXION=1 ZEP_LLM_ENTITY_RESOLVE=1
const BASE = process.env.SCENARIO_BASE_URL ?? "http://localhost:3002";
const EMAIL = `e2e+${Date.now()}@x.com`;
const PASSWORD = "hunter22hunter22";

type JSONObj = Record<string, unknown>;

async function req<T = JSONObj>(
  method: "GET" | "POST",
  path: string,
  body?: JSONObj,
  token?: string,
): Promise<T> {
  const r = await fetch(`${BASE}${path}`, {
    method,
    headers: {
      "content-type": "application/json",
      ...(token ? { authorization: `Bearer ${token}` } : {}),
    },
    body: body ? JSON.stringify(body) : undefined,
  });
  const text = await r.text();
  if (!r.ok) {
    throw new Error(`${method} ${path} → ${r.status}: ${text}`);
  }
  return text ? (JSON.parse(text) as T) : ({} as T);
}

function sleep(ms: number) {
  return new Promise((res) => setTimeout(res, ms));
}

const MESSAGES = [
  "I love dogs. I'm building an AI memory system at Grok in San Francisco.",
  "I love cats now, not dogs.",
  "Yesterday I met Karthik at the Anthropic office. He used to work at OpenAI on the Codex project.",
  "My friend Mei lives in Tokyo and runs a startup called Aria.",
  "I'm not building the memory system anymore.",
];

const QUERIES = [
  "what does the user love",
  "tell me about my friend in Japan",
  "Karthik's work history",
  "what is the user currently building",
];

async function pollUntilProcessed(
  episodeId: string,
  token: string,
  maxWaitMs = 90_000,
): Promise<"processed" | "failed"> {
  const start = Date.now();
  while (Date.now() - start < maxWaitMs) {
    const { episode } = (await req<{ episode: { status: string } }>(
      "GET",
      `/episodes/${episodeId}`,
      undefined,
      token,
    )) as { episode: { status: string } };
    if (episode.status === "processed" || episode.status === "failed") {
      return episode.status as "processed" | "failed";
    }
    await sleep(1500);
  }
  throw new Error(`episode ${episodeId} did not finish within ${maxWaitMs}ms`);
}

async function main() {
  console.log(`Base: ${BASE}`);
  console.log(`Flags in env:`);
  for (const name of [
    "ZEP_LLM_TEMPORAL",
    "ZEP_LLM_REFLEXION",
    "ZEP_LLM_ENTITY_RESOLVE",
    "ZEP_LLM_INVALIDATE",
    "ZEP_LLM_FACT_DEDUP",
  ]) {
    console.log(`  ${name}=${process.env[name] ?? "(unset)"}`);
  }
  console.log(`(note: the worker reads these, not this script)\n`);

  // signup + JWT
  console.log(`Signing up ${EMAIL}...`);
  const { token, userId } = (await req("POST", "/auth/signup", {
    email: EMAIL,
    password: PASSWORD,
  })) as { token: string; userId: string };
  console.log(`  userId=${userId}`);

  // session
  const { session } = (await req(
    "POST",
    "/sessions",
    { title: "scenario" },
    token,
  )) as { session: { id: string } };
  console.log(`  sessionId=${session.id}\n`);

  // ingest each message; space occurredAt 1 day apart so temporal ordering is clean
  const baseTime = new Date();
  baseTime.setUTCDate(baseTime.getUTCDate() - MESSAGES.length);

  const episodeIds: string[] = [];
  for (let i = 0; i < MESSAGES.length; i++) {
    const occurredAt = new Date(baseTime);
    occurredAt.setUTCDate(baseTime.getUTCDate() + i);
    occurredAt.setUTCHours(10, 0, 0, 0);

    console.log(
      `[ingest ${i + 1}/${MESSAGES.length} @ ${occurredAt.toISOString().slice(0, 16)}] "${MESSAGES[i]}"`,
    );
    const { episodeId } = (await req(
      "POST",
      "/ingest",
      {
        sessionId: session.id,
        actor: "user",
        content: MESSAGES[i],
        occurredAt: occurredAt.toISOString(),
      },
      token,
    )) as { episodeId: string };
    episodeIds.push(episodeId);
    console.log(`  → episodeId=${episodeId.slice(0, 8)}..`);
  }

  console.log(`\nWaiting for worker to process ${episodeIds.length} episodes...`);
  for (let i = 0; i < episodeIds.length; i++) {
    const status = await pollUntilProcessed(episodeIds[i], token);
    console.log(`  [${i + 1}] ${episodeIds[i].slice(0, 8)}.. → ${status}`);
    if (status === "failed") {
      const { logs } = (await req(
        "GET",
        `/episodes/${episodeIds[i]}`,
        undefined,
        token,
      )) as { logs: Array<{ step: string; status: string; message: string }> };
      for (const l of logs.filter((l) => l.status === "error")) {
        console.error(`    [ERR] ${l.step}: ${l.message}`);
      }
    }
  }

  // retrieve
  console.log(`\n${"=".repeat(72)}`);
  console.log(`RETRIEVAL`);
  console.log("=".repeat(72));

  for (const query of QUERIES) {
    console.log(`\n──── Q: ${query} ────`);
    const r = (await req(
      "POST",
      "/retrieve",
      { query, sessionId: session.id, limit: 6 },
      token,
    )) as {
      answer: string;
      facts: Array<{
        sourceName: string;
        relationType: string;
        targetName: string;
        factText: string;
        validFrom: string;
        validUntil: string | null;
        totalScore: number;
      }>;
    };
    console.log(`A: ${r.answer}`);
    console.log(`\nTop facts:`);
    if (r.facts.length === 0) {
      console.log(`  (none)`);
    } else {
      for (const f of r.facts) {
        const status = f.validUntil
          ? `closed @ ${f.validUntil.slice(0, 10)}`
          : "OPEN";
        console.log(
          `  [${f.totalScore.toFixed(2)}] ${f.sourceName} -[${f.relationType}]-> ${f.targetName} (${status})`,
        );
        console.log(`        "${f.factText}"`);
      }
    }
  }

  console.log(`\nDone. Inspect:\n  bun run verify`);
}

main().catch((err) => {
  console.error(err);
  process.exitCode = 1;
});

```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-12 15:20:59Z)**_

Yes — these are excellent test fixtures and the runner caught real behavior (good *and* bad). Here's what the graph dump shows:

## Verdict per ingestion message

| Msg | Path | Outcome |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `User-LIVES_IN-SF`, `User-LOVES-Dogs`, `User-WORKS_AT-Grok`, `User-BUILDING-AI memory system`. All open. | ✅ As expected |
| 2 | `User-LOVES-Cats` written. | ⚠️ **Bug surfaced** — see below |
| 3 | `Karthik-USED_TO_WORK_AT-OpenAI`, `Karthik-WORKED_ON-Codex`, `User-MET_AT-Karthik`. | ✅ All present (the LLM chose `USED_TO_WORK_AT` as the relationType instead of emitting `WORKS_AT`+invalidation — same end result) |
| 4 | `Mei-LIVES_IN-Tokyo`, `Mei-RUNS-Aria`. | ✅ Cleanly resolved |
| 5 | `User-BUILDING-AI memory system` **CLOSED @ 2026-05-11**. | ✅ Explicit invalidation worked end-to-end |

## Verdict per retrieval query

| Q | Outcome |
|---|---|
| 1 "what does the user love" | ⚠️ Answer is correct, top fact is wrong (see bug) |
| 2 "friend in Japan" | ✅ Multi-hop graph traversal nailed it (Mei + Aria) |
| 3 "Karthik's work history" | ✅ Right facts returned. ⚠️ LLM synthesizer hallucinated dates ("since 2021") — that's a `llmAnswer` prompt issue, not ingestion |
| 4 "currently building" | ✅ Perfect — closed fact correctly excluded by temporal filter, LLM gracefully says "I don't have information" |

## The Cats/Dogs bug

```
User -[LOVES]-> Dogs  (OPEN)
User -[LOVES]-> Cats  (CLOSED @ 2026-05-07T10:00:00Z)   ← inverted!
```

Cats is closed at 2026-05-07 (one day **before** message 2 even arrived). Dogs is still open. This is almost certainly a `ZEP_LLM_TEMPORAL=1` interaction: the temporal extraction LLM on the text `"The user loves cats now."` is mis-parsing "now" + the surrounding "not dogs" context and emitting a stale `validAt` and/or a non-null `invalidAt`. Then the rule-based contradiction path (which would have closed Dogs) never fires because the new Cats fact already arrives "closed" from the temporal-extraction step.

This is a real find — it's a flag interaction that the unit tests in `test:tier2` didn't catch because each test fixture used unambiguous temporal language. The "now, not dogs" phrasing is genuinely ambiguous and the LLM bites on it.

## What to do

Three options:

1. **Tighten the temporal prompt** — explicitly tell it "if validAt > invalidAt or invalidAt < reference timestamp, return nulls for both." That's a `extraction.service.ts` edit, ~5 lines in the system prompt.
2. **Add a sanity check in `applyTemporalFacts`** — after the LLM call, if `invalidAt !== null && invalidAt <= validAt`, drop both. That's a 3-line guard in `temporal.service.ts`.
3. **Turn off `ZEP_LLM_TEMPORAL`** for this conversation style. The flag helps when users say things like "two years ago"; it hurts when they say "now". Worth deciding per-deployment whether ambient temporal extraction is worth the cost.

Want me to apply fix #2 (the cheap defensive guard) and re-run the scenario to confirm? It's 3 lines, doesn't change behavior except in the degenerate case, and `test:tier2` will still pass since its fixtures don't hit this edge.

You can re-run the scenario any time with `bun run scenario` (with the server + worker already up). The script signs up a fresh user per run so it doesn't pollute prior state.

Fixes 1 and 2 are applied — but they don't fix the symptom because the symptom isn't actually an LLM mis-parse. The actual cause is a **worker concurrency race**, revealed by the timestamps:

```
Cats:  tCreated = 15:27:52.80 → tExpired = 15:28:07.36  (closed 15 sec after creation)
Dogs:  tCreated = 15:28:07.53                            (created 200 ms after Cats was closed)
```

Episodes are processed in parallel (`concurrency: 4`). ep2 ("loves cats now") finished first and wrote Cats at `validFrom=2026-05-08`. ep1 ("loves dogs") was still running its LLM extraction. When ep1 finally reached the rule-based contradiction step, it saw the newly-written Cats edge in its `(User, LOVES)` group, and — because Cats isn't in ep1's new-targets — it closed Cats using ep1's own `occurredAt=2026-05-07`. That's why `validUntil < validFrom` on the Cats edge.
